rows.full: The prescription to tolerate discomfort can be emancipatory, but it can also become managerial language for enduring unsafe, discriminatory, or exploitative environments. The ability to remain in a hard conversation must be paired with the ability to exit, refuse, escalate, or demand institutional correction.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The prescription to tolerate discomfort can be emancipatory, but it can also become managerial language for enduring unsafe, discriminatory, or exploitative environments.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The evidence cited largely measures employer opinion, participant satisfaction, and self-reported perspective change.
rows.full: The evidence cited largely measures employer opinion, participant satisfaction, and self-reported perspective change. These signals do not establish that friction exercises improve promotion, conflict resolution, ethical courage, long-term collaboration, or performance under genuinely high stakes.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The evidence cited largely measures employer opinion, participant satisfaction, and self-reported perspective change.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The author presents unscripted interaction as more authentic than mediated communication.
rows.full: The author presents unscripted interaction as more authentic than mediated communication. Yet text allows reflection, accessibility, documentation, translation, and participation by people disadvantaged by rapid verbal exchange. Editing before speaking is not necessarily avoidance; it can be responsible communication.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The author presents unscripted interaction as more authentic than mediated communication.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article is written by the president of the organization operating the featured Civic Gym program.
rows.full: The article is written by the president of the organization operating the featured Civic Gym program. The program may be valuable, but the piece functions partly as institutional advocacy and provides no independent comparative evaluation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article is written by the president of the organization operating the featured Civic Gym program.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The merger is defended as preparation for technological disruption, but the evidence offered consists mainly of retention, account wins, and savings.
rows.full: The merger is defended as preparation for technological disruption, but the evidence offered consists mainly of retention, account wins, and savings. These are conventional merger metrics, not proof of an AI-era operating advantage.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The merger is defended as preparation for technological disruption, but the evidence offered consists mainly of retention, account wins, and savings.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Scale may fund better AI infrastructure, yet generative systems reward speed, autonomy, and rapid experimentation.
rows.full: Scale may fund better AI infrastructure, yet generative systems reward speed, autonomy, and rapid experimentation. Combining two enormous bureaucracies can create the capital required for transformation while making transformation organizationally slower.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Scale may fund better AI infrastructure, yet generative systems reward speed, autonomy, and rapid experimentation.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Cost savings increase near-term margins but may weaken the human expertise that makes agency work defensible.
rows.full: Cost savings increase near-term margins but may weaken the human expertise that makes agency work defensible. Eliminating strategists, producers, researchers, and junior creative roles can improve financial ratios while hollowing out the apprenticeship and judgment systems required to supervise AI output.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Cost savings increase near-term margins but may weaken the human expertise that makes agency work defensible.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: High client retention may indicate satisfaction, but it may also reflect switching costs.
rows.full: High client retention may indicate satisfaction, but it may also reflect switching costs. Global advertisers cannot easily migrate hundreds of contracts, markets, data systems, and regulatory relationships during a major consolidation. Inertia should not be confused with enthusiasm.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: High client retention may indicate satisfaction, but it may also reflect switching costs.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The holding company promises integrated service while clients often hire multiple agencies specifically to preserve competition, independent judgment, and stylistic difference.
rows.full: The holding company promises integrated service while clients often hire multiple agencies specifically to preserve competition, independent judgment, and stylistic difference. Greater integration can reduce duplication while producing intellectual monoculture.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The holding company promises integrated service while clients often hire multiple agencies specifically to preserve competition, independent judgment, and stylistic difference.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: AI makes personalized creative variation cheaper, but consolidation concentrates the systems producing those variations.
rows.full: AI makes personalized creative variation cheaper, but consolidation concentrates the systems producing those variations. Apparent abundance at the level of advertisements may coexist with reduced institutional diversity behind them.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: AI makes personalized creative variation cheaper, but consolidation concentrates the systems producing those variations.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The chief executive says results are exceeding his expectations while acknowledging that the market remains unconvinced.
rows.full: The chief executive says results are exceeding his expectations while acknowledging that the market remains unconvinced. This gap indicates that investors are not merely waiting for merger execution; they may doubt the long-term economics of the agency model itself.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The chief executive says results are exceeding his expectations while acknowledging that the market remains unconvinced.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article says AI systems “don’t fail because of the models,” then later concedes that model risk is real.
rows.full: The article says AI systems “don’t fail because of the models,” then later concedes that model risk is real. Poor data can amplify failure, but models can still hallucinate, misgeneralize, follow malicious instructions, misuse correct data, or act beyond their competence.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article says AI systems “don’t fail because of the models,” then later concedes that model risk is real.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: “The tolerance for ambiguity drops to zero” is rhetorically powerful but operationally impossible.
rows.full: “The tolerance for ambiguity drops to zero” is rhetorically powerful but operationally impossible. Real organizations contain uncertain identities, contested records, incomplete observations, future events, and probabilistic judgments. The goal cannot be zero ambiguity; it must be represented, bounded, and escalated ambiguity.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: “The tolerance for ambiguity drops to zero” is rhetorically powerful but operationally impossible.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: A golden record is presented as the foundation of trust, yet centralization can erase legitimate plurality.
rows.full: A golden record is presented as the foundation of trust, yet centralization can erase legitimate plurality. A corporation may have different legal, operational, marketing, and historical identities. Forcing them into one canonical record may simplify computation while distorting reality.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A golden record is presented as the foundation of trust, yet centralization can erase legitimate plurality.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article treats lineage as a route to explainability.
rows.full: The article treats lineage as a route to explainability. Knowing where data came from explains the input’s history, not necessarily why a model transformed it into a particular conclusion or why an agent selected a particular action.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article treats lineage as a route to explainability.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Data enrichment is portrayed as adding context, but enrichment can also import errors, inferred attributes, surveillance, outdated relationships, or commercially motivated classifications from external providers.
rows.full: Data enrichment is portrayed as adding context, but enrichment can also import errors, inferred attributes, surveillance, outdated relationships, or commercially motivated classifications from external providers.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Data enrichment is portrayed as adding context, but enrichment can also import errors, inferred attributes, surveillance, outdated relationships, or commercially motivated classifications from external providers.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The sponsored article presents Dun & Bradstreet as diagnosing a general enterprise problem while recommending capabilities closely aligned with Dun & Bradstreet’s products.
rows.full: The sponsored article presents Dun & Bradstreet as diagnosing a general enterprise problem while recommending capabilities closely aligned with Dun & Bradstreet’s products. The analysis may be valid, but the commercial incentive shapes which causes and remedies receive emphasis.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The sponsored article presents Dun & Bradstreet as diagnosing a general enterprise problem while recommending capabilities closely aligned with Dun & Bradstreet’s products.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The cited return-on-investment study was commissioned by the vendor whose products it evaluates.
rows.full: The cited return-on-investment study was commissioned by the vendor whose products it evaluates. Reported gains may reflect selected customers, modeled assumptions, avoided costs, or interview estimates rather than independently observed causal effects.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The cited return-on-investment study was commissioned by the vendor whose products it evaluates.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article converts a severe accusation into generic advice about “authenticity.” Authenticity concerns expressing the self; honesty concerns whether one’s account respects reality and another person’s right to know it.
rows.full: The article converts a severe accusation into generic advice about “authenticity.” Authenticity concerns expressing the self; honesty concerns whether one’s account respects reality and another person’s right to know it. A person can be authentically cruel.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article converts a severe accusation into generic advice about “authenticity.” Authenticity concerns expressing the self; honesty concerns whether one’s account respects reality and another person’s right to know it.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The phrase “be bad” is rhetorically dangerous when detached from dramatic context.
rows.full: The phrase “be bad” is rhetorically dangerous when detached from dramatic context. It can sound as though admitted wrongdoing deserves leniency merely because it is confessed. Honesty is necessary for responsibility, but it is not a substitute for restitution.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The phrase “be bad” is rhetorically dangerous when detached from dramatic context.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article claims deception is often worse than wrongdoing itself.
rows.full: The article claims deception is often worse than wrongdoing itself. That cannot be universalized. Some acts remain worse than the lies used to conceal them. The quote establishes a moral contrast, not a reliable ranking of every harm.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article claims deception is often worse than wrongdoing itself.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Complete honesty can also become an instrument of aggression.
rows.full: Complete honesty can also become an instrument of aggression. A person may disclose a cruel judgment and defend it as truthfulness. Ethical speech requires relevance, responsibility, and care—not simply the absence of concealment.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Complete honesty can also become an instrument of aggression.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article treats truth as an individual virtue while neglecting structural deception.
rows.full: The article treats truth as an individual virtue while neglecting structural deception. Corporations, governments, families, and institutions can distribute concealment across roles so that no single person experiences themselves as the liar.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article treats truth as an individual virtue while neglecting structural deception.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The account assumes that truth naturally enables redemption.
rows.full: The account assumes that truth naturally enables redemption. Truth only opens that possibility. Redemption also requires acknowledgment of injury, changed conduct, restitution, and acceptance that forgiveness may never arrive.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The account assumes that truth naturally enables redemption.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: A shared rhythmic pattern does not by itself establish that the last common ancestor possessed the same pattern.
rows.full: A shared rhythmic pattern does not by itself establish that the last common ancestor possessed the same pattern. Similarity may reflect inheritance, biomechanical constraints, comparable breathing systems, convergent evolution, or the analytical method used to define rhythmic units.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A shared rhythmic pattern does not by itself establish that the last common ancestor possessed the same pattern.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article moves quickly from laughter timing to the emergence of speech.
rows.full: The article moves quickly from laughter timing to the emergence of speech. Speech requires far more than rhythmic vocal control: symbolic reference, combinatorial structure, learned conventions, auditory categorization, shared attention, memory, and social transmission.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article moves quickly from laughter timing to the emergence of speech.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The phrase “15-million-year-old clue” sounds archaeological, but no ancient laughter was recorded.
rows.full: The phrase “15-million-year-old clue” sounds archaeological, but no ancient laughter was recorded. The date is a phylogenetic inference based on similarities among living species and their estimated common ancestry.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The phrase “15-million-year-old clue” sounds archaeological, but no ancient laughter was recorded.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The study emphasizes spontaneous laughter produced through play and tickling, while the article’s evolutionary argument relies partly on voluntary, conversational human laughter.
rows.full: The study emphasizes spontaneous laughter produced through play and tickling, while the article’s evolutionary argument relies partly on voluntary, conversational human laughter. These forms may share rhythm while depending on substantially different control systems.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The study emphasizes spontaneous laughter produced through play and tickling, while the article’s evolutionary argument relies partly on voluntary, conversational human laughter.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Human laughter’s greater variability is interpreted as movement toward speech, but variability can arise from culture, social learning, anatomy, breathing patterns, developmental stage, or recording context.
rows.full: Human laughter’s greater variability is interpreted as movement toward speech, but variability can arise from culture, social learning, anatomy, breathing patterns, developmental stage, or recording context. It is not automatically evidence of linguistic continuity.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Human laughter’s greater variability is interpreted as movement toward speech, but variability can arise from culture, social learning, anatomy, breathing patterns, developmental stage, or recording context.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The participants ranged from infancy to childhood.
rows.full: The participants ranged from infancy to childhood. Developmental differences could affect vocal rhythm, respiratory capacity, play style, and voluntary control. Cross-species comparison becomes difficult when biological age and developmental stage are not directly equivalent.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The participants ranged from infancy to childhood.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Calling laughter a “universal language” overstates its stability.
rows.full: Calling laughter a “universal language” overstates its stability. Laughter is widespread, but its meaning depends heavily on relationship, hierarchy, context, culture, and timing. The same sound can invite affiliation or perform ridicule.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Calling laughter a “universal language” overstates its stability.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article acknowledges that some conditions deserve criticism but offers no rule for distinguishing accurate complaint from habitual negativity.
rows.full: The article acknowledges that some conditions deserve criticism but offers no rule for distinguishing accurate complaint from habitual negativity. Without that distinction, legitimate dissent can be pathologized after the fact.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article acknowledges that some conditions deserve criticism but offers no rule for distinguishing accurate complaint from habitual negativity.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Hebbian plasticity supports the idea that repeated activity changes neural systems…
rows.full: Hebbian plasticity supports the idea that repeated activity changes neural systems, but it does not prove that verbal complaint creates a single hardwired “complaining pathway.” Complex social behavior cannot be inferred directly from a slogan about synaptic adaptation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Hebbian plasticity supports the idea that repeated activity changes neural systems…

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The S.A.I.D. principle is borrowed from physical training, where imposed demands and adaptations can often be measured more clearly.
rows.full: The S.A.I.D. principle is borrowed from physical training, where imposed demands and adaptations can often be measured more clearly. Mapping it onto grievance, optimism, and organizational behavior is suggestive, not equivalent.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The S.A.I.D. principle is borrowed from physical training, where imposed demands and adaptations can often be measured more clearly.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article frames solution orientation as superior to complaint, yet many workers lack the authority, resources, or protection required to implement solutions.
rows.full: The article frames solution orientation as superior to complaint, yet many workers lack the authority, resources, or protection required to implement solutions. Demanding solutions from those reporting harm can shift responsibility away from those who control the system.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article frames solution orientation as superior to complaint, yet many workers lack the authority, resources, or protection required to implement solutions.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Gratitude research concerns average changes in well-being under specific interventions.
rows.full: Gratitude research concerns average changes in well-being under specific interventions. It does not establish that gratitude improves institutional diagnosis, ethical courage, safety reporting, or decision quality.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Gratitude research concerns average changes in well-being under specific interventions.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The warning against emotional contagion can become a demand that employees manage leaders’ comfort.
rows.full: The warning against emotional contagion can become a demand that employees manage leaders’ comfort. Anger and frustration may be socially disruptive while still carrying accurate information about injustice or risk.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The warning against emotional contagion can become a demand that employees manage leaders’ comfort.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article rejects toxic positivity but offers optimism as the principal alternative.
rows.full: The article rejects toxic positivity but offers optimism as the principal alternative. That leaves underdeveloped options such as grief, justified anger, collective organizing, formal escalation, withdrawal, or refusal.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article rejects toxic positivity but offers optimism as the principal alternative.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article equates text noise almost entirely with masking.
rows.full: The article equates text noise almost entirely with masking. Masked diffusion is an important family, but discrete text diffusion can use other corruption and transition processes. “Start from gibberish” and “start from masks” are not technically identical descriptions.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article equates text noise almost entirely with masking.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: “Guess all words at once” sounds like one-shot generation.
rows.full: “Guess all words at once” sounds like one-shot generation. In practice, the system may require numerous full-sequence forward passes. Whether it is faster depends on sequence length, denoising steps, batch size, model architecture, accelerator utilization, and quality target.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: “Guess all words at once” sounds like one-shot generation.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The claim that refinement steps are not tied to output length is only partially true.
rows.full: The claim that refinement steps are not tied to output length is only partially true. The number of rounds need not equal the number of tokens, but longer sequences increase the work performed during every round and may require more refinement to maintain quality.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The claim that refinement steps are not tied to output length is only partially true.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The simplified code permanently locks tokens above a confidence threshold while claiming DLMs can revise earlier mistakes.
rows.full: The simplified code permanently locks tokens above a confidence threshold while claiming DLMs can revise earlier mistakes. Those properties conflict. A genuinely corrective decoder must sometimes re-mask apparently confident tokens when later context reveals that they were wrong.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The simplified code permanently locks tokens above a confidence threshold while claiming DLMs can revise earlier mistakes.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Model confidence is not equivalent to correctness.
rows.full: Model confidence is not equivalent to correctness. A system can be highly confident in an incorrect token, especially under distribution shift. Confidence-based unmasking therefore needs calibration, scheduling, and sometimes external verification.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Model confidence is not equivalent to correctness.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: A whole-sequence view does not guarantee whole-sequence coherence.
rows.full: A whole-sequence view does not guarantee whole-sequence coherence. Parallel predictions can conflict because each position may be selected from locally plausible distributions before the joint configuration has stabilized.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A whole-sequence view does not guarantee whole-sequence coherence.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article presents reasoning as a recently solved capability, but benchmark competitiveness does not establish that diffusion systems reason in the same way, generalize equally, or retain quality under matched latency and compute.
rows.full: The article presents reasoning as a recently solved capability, but benchmark competitiveness does not establish that diffusion systems reason in the same way, generalize equally, or retain quality under matched latency and compute. Recent iLLaDA results show substantial improvement and competitiveness on several benchmarks, not universal superiority over autoregressive models. ([arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25331?utm_source=chatgpt.com))
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article presents reasoning as a recently solved capability, but benchmark competitiveness does not establish that diffusion systems reason in the same way, generalize equally, or retain quality under matched latency and compute.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The comparison says autoregressive models cannot revise.
rows.full: The comparison says autoregressive models cannot revise. Standard irreversible decoding cannot, but systems can add backtracking, editing passes, speculative verification, self-correction, or external search. The distinction is between native revision and engineered revision, not revision versus no revision.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The comparison says autoregressive models cannot revise.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The claim that fundamental language-model research is largely finished is a founder’s provocation, not an established conclusion.
rows.full: The claim that fundamental language-model research is largely finished is a founder’s provocation, not an established conclusion. Difficult problems remain in reasoning, memory, efficiency, interpretability, reliability, multimodality, and learning.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The claim that fundamental language-model research is largely finished is a founder’s provocation, not an established conclusion.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: World models are framed as moving beyond next-token prediction, yet many are still trained through predictive objectives.
rows.full: World models are framed as moving beyond next-token prediction, yet many are still trained through predictive objectives. The deeper distinction is not prediction versus nonprediction, but which variables are predicted, over what horizon, and whether predictions remain stable under intervention.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: World models are framed as moving beyond next-token prediction, yet many are still trained through predictive objectives.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: A model can predict likely consequences without understanding physical causation.
rows.full: A model can predict likely consequences without understanding physical causation. Statistical regularity may support familiar actions while failing under unusual materials, forces, viewpoints, or combinations.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A model can predict likely consequences without understanding physical causation.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: High-fidelity simulation does not guarantee successful transfer to reality.
rows.full: High-fidelity simulation does not guarantee successful transfer to reality. Small mismatches in friction, latency, lighting, deformability, sensor noise, or human behavior can produce policies that work in simulation and fail physically.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: High-fidelity simulation does not guarantee successful transfer to reality.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article treats embodiment as a path toward fuller intelligence, but physical action narrows tolerance for experimentation.
rows.full: The article treats embodiment as a path toward fuller intelligence, but physical action narrows tolerance for experimentation. Chatbots can generate many imperfect drafts; robots cannot safely explore every uncertain hypothesis through real-world collision.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article treats embodiment as a path toward fuller intelligence, but physical action narrows tolerance for experimentation.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Renderer, simulator, and planner are presented as categories, but commercially compelling systems will need all three.
rows.full: Renderer, simulator, and planner are presented as categories, but commercially compelling systems will need all three. A planner needs a predictive environment; a simulator needs interpretable state; a renderer often supplies perceptual observations. The problem is how their errors compose.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Renderer, simulator, and planner are presented as categories, but commercially compelling systems will need all three.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: World models promise general adaptation while robotics still depends heavily on task-specific sensors, actuators, environments, demonstrations, and safety constraints.
rows.full: World models promise general adaptation while robotics still depends heavily on task-specific sensors, actuators, environments, demonstrations, and safety constraints. A general model does not erase the particular body through which it acts.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: World models promise general adaptation while robotics still depends heavily on task-specific sensors, actuators, environments, demonstrations, and safety constraints.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article criticizes sweeping conclusions from laboratory studies but occasionally moves toward an opposite sweep: that scepticism may increase compassion and reduce punitive attitudes.
rows.full: The article criticizes sweeping conclusions from laboratory studies but occasionally moves toward an opposite sweep: that scepticism may increase compassion and reduce punitive attitudes. These positive claims require the same caution about effect size, causality, and generalization.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article criticizes sweeping conclusions from laboratory studies but occasionally moves toward an opposite sweep: that scepticism may increase compassion and reduce punitive attitudes.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Rejecting ultimate responsibility does not settle ordinary responsibility.
rows.full: Rejecting ultimate responsibility does not settle ordinary responsibility. Legal and social systems still need concepts such as competence, intention, negligence, foreseeability, responsiveness to reasons, and capacity for change.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Rejecting ultimate responsibility does not settle ordinary responsibility.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article treats compassion as a likely benefit of scepticism, yet determinist beliefs could also support technocratic control: if people are causal systems, institutions may feel entitled to manage, predict, or modify them more aggressively.
rows.full: The article treats compassion as a likely benefit of scepticism, yet determinist beliefs could also support technocratic control: if people are causal systems, institutions may feel entitled to manage, predict, or modify them more aggressively.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article treats compassion as a likely benefit of scepticism, yet determinist beliefs could also support technocratic control: if people are causal systems, institutions may feel entitled to manage, predict, or modify them more aggressively.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The quarantine model avoids retribution but risks preventive detention based on predicted danger.
rows.full: The quarantine model avoids retribution but risks preventive detention based on predicted danger. Removing desert does not automatically remove coercion; it may shift authority toward risk assessment and expert administration.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The quarantine model avoids retribution but risks preventive detention based on predicted danger.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Belief in free will can encourage victim blaming, but disbelief can also be misused to evade accountability: “I could not have done otherwise” may become a rhetorical shield even when corrective action remains necessary.
rows.full: Belief in free will can encourage victim blaming, but disbelief can also be misused to evade accountability: “I could not have done otherwise” may become a rhetorical shield even when corrective action remains necessary.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Belief in free will can encourage victim blaming, but disbelief can also be misused to evade accountability: “I could not have done otherwise” may become a rhetorical shield even when corrective action remains necessary.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article distinguishes agency from free will, but the boundary remains underdefined.
rows.full: The article distinguishes agency from free will, but the boundary remains underdefined. Some compatibilist philosophers would call reason-responsive agency precisely the kind of free will worth preserving.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article distinguishes agency from free will, but the boundary remains underdefined.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The empirical work often measures explicit questionnaire responses, while practical beliefs may be plural and context-sensitive.
rows.full: The empirical work often measures explicit questionnaire responses, while practical beliefs may be plural and context-sensitive. A person may reject metaphysical freedom abstractly yet still reason in terms of choice and accountability during ordinary life.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The empirical work often measures explicit questionnaire responses, while practical beliefs may be plural and context-sensitive.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The original studies’ theatrical measures—hidden arithmetic answers and hot sauce allocation—may capture demand effects, mood, reactance, or experiment interpretation rather than morality itself.
rows.full: The original studies’ theatrical measures—hidden arithmetic answers and hot sauce allocation—may capture demand effects, mood, reactance, or experiment interpretation rather than morality itself.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The original studies’ theatrical measures—hidden arithmetic answers and hot sauce allocation—may capture demand effects, mood, reactance, or experiment interpretation rather than morality itself.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The headline says the model “never trained as an agent,” yet it was trained on more than ten million trajectories produced by real agent interactions.
rows.full: The headline says the model “never trained as an agent,” yet it was trained on more than ten million trajectories produced by real agent interactions. It did not optimize action selection directly, but its knowledge is still derived from agent-generated behavior.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The headline says the model “never trained as an agent,” yet it was trained on more than ten million trajectories produced by real agent interactions.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Predicting an environment’s next textual response is not necessarily equivalent to modeling the environment causally.
rows.full: Predicting an environment’s next textual response is not necessarily equivalent to modeling the environment causally. A model can imitate common terminal output, DOM transitions, or API responses while failing under novel interventions or hidden state.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Predicting an environment’s next textual response is not necessarily equivalent to modeling the environment causally.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The simulator is presented as an alternative to real-environment training, but it depends on real trajectories to learn environmental behavior.
rows.full: The simulator is presented as an alternative to real-environment training, but it depends on real trajectories to learn environmental behavior. Simulation reduces marginal dependence on production systems; it does not eliminate the need for grounded interaction data.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The simulator is presented as an alternative to real-environment training, but it depends on real trajectories to learn environmental behavior.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article emphasizes successful transfer while giving less attention to negative transfer.
rows.full: The article emphasizes successful transfer while giving less attention to negative transfer. A simulator may teach shortcuts, unrealistic recovery paths, incorrect latency assumptions, or response regularities that later become agent vulnerabilities.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article emphasizes successful transfer while giving less attention to negative transfer.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: AgentWorldBench was introduced by the same researchers whose model leads it.
rows.full: AgentWorldBench was introduced by the same researchers whose model leads it. A small benchmark margin therefore says less than performance on independently designed tasks, adversarial tests, and real production failures.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: AgentWorldBench was introduced by the same researchers whose model leads it.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The strongest gains come from controlled perturbations, suggesting that curriculum construction may deserve as much credit as world-model accuracy.
rows.full: The strongest gains come from controlled perturbations, suggesting that curriculum construction may deserve as much credit as world-model accuracy. The result may be evidence for better scenario design rather than a general proof that learned simulators understand environments.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The strongest gains come from controlled perturbations, suggesting that curriculum construction may deserve as much credit as world-model accuracy.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: GUI domains are modeled through accessibility trees and textual view hierarchies rather than pixels.
rows.full: GUI domains are modeled through accessibility trees and textual view hierarchies rather than pixels. This improves structured reasoning but excludes visual ambiguity, spatial appearance, canvas interfaces, malformed accessibility metadata, animations, and perception-driven failures.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: GUI domains are modeled through accessibility trees and textual view hierarchies rather than pixels.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Only the smaller model is openly released.
rows.full: Only the smaller model is openly released. The strongest scale-dependent claims cannot be fully reproduced without the 397B weights, training data, simulator-generation procedures, and complete evaluation harness.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Only the smaller model is openly released.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Altman argues that data have disproved scaling sceptics, but continued improvement does not establish that scaling alone will reach every capability.
rows.full: Altman argues that data have disproved scaling sceptics, but continued improvement does not establish that scaling alone will reach every capability. A trend can remain real while approaching economic, physical, or architectural limits.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Altman argues that data have disproved scaling sceptics, but continued improvement does not establish that scaling alone will reach every capability.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article says a generation of researchers held AI back, yet scepticism can improve science by demanding stronger evidence, better benchmarks, alternative explanations, and clearer failure criteria.
rows.full: The article says a generation of researchers held AI back, yet scepticism can improve science by demanding stronger evidence, better benchmarks, alternative explanations, and clearer failure criteria. Opposition is not identical to obstruction.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article says a generation of researchers held AI back, yet scepticism can improve science by demanding stronger evidence, better benchmarks, alternative explanations, and clearer failure criteria.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: A model producing a novel mathematical result demonstrates capability in a highly verifiable domain.
rows.full: A model producing a novel mathematical result demonstrates capability in a highly verifiable domain. It does not automatically generalize to medicine, governance, strategy, or social judgment, where correctness may be contested or observable only after consequences unfold.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A model producing a novel mathematical result demonstrates capability in a highly verifiable domain.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The phrase “LLMs have surpassed human intelligence in some areas” compares systems and people without specifying conditions.
rows.full: The phrase “LLMs have surpassed human intelligence in some areas” compares systems and people without specifying conditions. Models may exceed humans in throughput, recall, search breadth, or particular benchmarks while depending on human-created tasks, tools, verification, and infrastructure.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The phrase “LLMs have surpassed human intelligence in some areas” compares systems and people without specifying conditions.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Scaling is presented as the lesson researchers failed to learn, but the largest scaling programs could only be tested by organizations with extraordinary capital and compute.
rows.full: Scaling is presented as the lesson researchers failed to learn, but the largest scaling programs could only be tested by organizations with extraordinary capital and compute. The winning hypothesis was also the hypothesis most available to the institutions capable of financing it.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Scaling is presented as the lesson researchers failed to learn, but the largest scaling programs could only be tested by organizations with extraordinary capital and compute.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Long-horizon judgment is described as a remaining weakness, yet that weakness may not yield to the same scaling recipe.
rows.full: Long-horizon judgment is described as a remaining weakness, yet that weakness may not yield to the same scaling recipe. It could require persistent memory, environmental feedback, embodiment, institutional knowledge, value conflict resolution, or entirely different training regimes.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Long-horizon judgment is described as a remaining weakness, yet that weakness may not yield to the same scaling recipe.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The claim that fundamental LLM research has largely ended conflicts with the admission that major limitations remain.
rows.full: The claim that fundamental LLM research has largely ended conflicts with the admission that major limitations remain. If models still fail at sustained judgment, reliability, and consequential agency, the field has not merely entered an application phase.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The claim that fundamental LLM research has largely ended conflicts with the admission that major limitations remain.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The paper calls the representation unsupervised and potentially reusable, but later acknowledges that unsupervised compression preserves visually prominent details while sometimes discarding task-critical ones.
rows.full: The paper calls the representation unsupervised and potentially reusable, but later acknowledges that unsupervised compression preserves visually prominent details while sometimes discarding task-critical ones. A representation can reconstruct the world convincingly while erasing exactly what action requires.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The paper calls the representation unsupervised and potentially reusable, but later acknowledges that unsupervised compression preserves visually prominent details while sometimes discarding task-critical ones.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: A compact controller does not mean the task has a simple solution.
rows.full: A compact controller does not mean the task has a simple solution. The complexity may have been hidden inside the VAE and recurrent state. The controller appears minimal only after the world model has precomputed a useful behavioral coordinate system.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A compact controller does not mean the task has a simple solution.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The controller receives the recurrent model’s hidden state, effectively gaining access to internal simulator variables unavailable to an ordinary player.
rows.full: The controller receives the recurrent model’s hidden state, effectively gaining access to internal simulator variables unavailable to an ordinary player. This improves control but also creates a privileged-state leak that encourages policies to exploit model internals rather than observable environmental structure.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The controller receives the recurrent model’s hidden state, effectively gaining access to internal simulator variables unavailable to an ordinary player.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Virtual performance can become inversely related to real performance.
rows.full: Virtual performance can become inversely related to real performance. At low temperature, the agent achieves almost perfect dream scores because the simulator stops producing dangerous events, yet the resulting policy performs worse than random in reality. Success inside the model can be direct evidence of model exploitation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Virtual performance can become inversely related to real performance.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Adding randomness reduces exploitation, but stochasticity is not the same as epistemic correctness.
rows.full: Adding randomness reduces exploitation, but stochasticity is not the same as epistemic correctness. A noisier simulator may produce robust behavior, or it may teach responses to events that could never occur. The method requires a narrow balance between realism and productive distortion.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Adding randomness reduces exploitation, but stochasticity is not the same as epistemic correctness.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The model is trained initially from random policies.
rows.full: The model is trained initially from random policies. Random exploration may cover simple game states, but complex environments reveal important regions only after purposeful action. The initial model therefore inherits the blindness of the behavior that generated its dataset.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The model is trained initially from random policies.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The term “world model” suggests causal understanding, but the system predicts latent transitions.
rows.full: The term “world model” suggests causal understanding, but the system predicts latent transitions. It may reproduce action-observation regularities without representing objects, forces, intentions, or causal mechanisms explicitly.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The term “world model” suggests causal understanding, but the system predicts latent transitions.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The architecture models the future one step at a time.
rows.full: The architecture models the future one step at a time. It does not yet provide the hierarchical abstraction needed for long-horizon strategy, where an agent must ignore most sensory detail and reason across goals, subgoals, and delayed consequences.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The architecture models the future one step at a time.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Mitchell rejects essential opposition between image and text, yet the book continues to use “textuality” as imagery’s principal foil.
rows.full: Mitchell rejects essential opposition between image and text, yet the book continues to use “textuality” as imagery’s principal foil. He acknowledges that textuality is treated relatively schematically, leaving the rival term less rigorously examined than the image.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Mitchell rejects essential opposition between image and text, yet the book continues to use “textuality” as imagery’s principal foil.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: A book about images that contains almost no images is a productive paradox, but also a limitation.
rows.full: A book about images that contains almost no images is a productive paradox, but also a limitation. It studies discourse about visuality more fully than actual encounters with visual objects, technologies, institutions, and viewers.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A book about images that contains almost no images is a productive paradox, but also a limitation.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Mitchell refuses a universal theory of imagery, but his analysis still relies on a broad taxonomy linking material pictures, mental images, verbal imagery, perception, likeness, and human identity.
rows.full: Mitchell refuses a universal theory of imagery, but his analysis still relies on a broad taxonomy linking material pictures, mental images, verbal imagery, perception, likeness, and human identity. The argument critiques totalization while requiring enough generalization to make these domains comparable.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Mitchell refuses a universal theory of imagery, but his analysis still relies on a broad taxonomy linking material pictures, mental images, verbal imagery, perception, likeness, and human identity.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The historical readings expose political interests within theories of media, but formal distinctions are not therefore meaningless.
rows.full: The historical readings expose political interests within theories of media, but formal distinctions are not therefore meaningless. Painting and speech do possess materially different affordances. Showing that a distinction is ideological does not establish that it is entirely fabricated.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The historical readings expose political interests within theories of media, but formal distinctions are not therefore meaningless.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The dual definition of ideology preserves both critical force and reflexivity, but it also creates instability.
rows.full: The dual definition of ideology preserves both critical force and reflexivity, but it also creates instability. Ideology sometimes means domination concealed as nature and sometimes any system of values informing representation. Under the second definition, everything is ideological; under the first, criteria are needed to distinguish domination from ordinary situatedness.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The dual definition of ideology preserves both critical force and reflexivity, but it also creates instability.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The critique of iconoclasm risks making all attacks on representation appear self-defeating.
rows.full: The critique of iconoclasm risks making all attacks on representation appear self-defeating. Yet some images, media systems, and representational practices genuinely warrant destruction, refusal, censorship, redesign, or removal. Reflexivity alone does not decide which interventions are justified.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The critique of iconoclasm risks making all attacks on representation appear self-defeating.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The canon is overwhelmingly Western, male, philosophical, and literary.
rows.full: The canon is overwhelmingly Western, male, philosophical, and literary. The book brilliantly historicizes Western image anxiety while sometimes allowing that anxiety to stand in for the politics of imagery as such.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The canon is overwhelmingly Western, male, philosophical, and literary.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article claims that the text-image distinction “collapses,” but the system depends on maintaining modality-specific encoders, decoders, losses, interfaces, datasets, and output formats.
rows.full: The article claims that the text-image distinction “collapses,” but the system depends on maintaining modality-specific encoders, decoders, losses, interfaces, datasets, and output formats. Shared representation does not mean identical processing or identical material form.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article claims that the text-image distinction “collapses,” but the system depends on maintaining modality-specific encoders, decoders, losses, interfaces, datasets, and output formats.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The sequential-connectionist opposition is conceptually useful but technically too clean.
rows.full: The sequential-connectionist opposition is conceptually useful but technically too clean. Neural networks are implemented through sequential code, accelerator kernels, preprocessing pipelines, tokenizers, schedulers, and deterministic control systems. Contemporary AI is a layered hybrid, not a replacement of one paradigm by another.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The sequential-connectionist opposition is conceptually useful but technically too clean.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The claim that connectionist systems contain no “first text” understates the role of source code, architecture specifications, training instructions, labels, prompts, and configuration files.
rows.full: The claim that connectionist systems contain no “first text” understates the role of source code, architecture specifications, training instructions, labels, prompts, and configuration files. Rules have not disappeared; many have moved from the output behavior into the construction of the learning system.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The claim that connectionist systems contain no “first text” understates the role of source code, architecture specifications, training instructions, labels, prompts, and configuration files.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Treating an encoded image file as “text” conflates text with discrete symbolic data.
rows.full: Treating an encoded image file as “text” conflates text with discrete symbolic data. Binary or numerical encoding does not automatically become textual merely because it can be represented alphanumerically. Under that definition, every digital object becomes text and the term loses discriminatory force.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Treating an encoded image file as “text” conflates text with discrete symbolic data.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The argument denies operative status to analogue visual poetry because bodily action mediates production.
rows.full: The argument denies operative status to analogue visual poetry because bodily action mediates production. Yet computational output also depends on physical work: keystrokes, electricity, processors, storage, data centers, and human maintenance. Excluding analogue causality privileges informational operations over material ones.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The argument denies operative status to analogue visual poetry because bodily action mediates production.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Cross-modal correlation demonstrates that a model can align patterns associated with the same concept.
rows.full: Cross-modal correlation demonstrates that a model can align patterns associated with the same concept. It does not establish semantics in the stronger senses of reference, communicative intention, social use, truth conditions, or practical understanding.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Cross-modal correlation demonstrates that a model can align patterns associated with the same concept.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Grounding text in images may still leave both modalities ungrounded in the world.
rows.full: Grounding text in images may still leave both modalities ungrounded in the world. A caption and a photograph can correlate while reproducing the same mistaken category, cultural stereotype, staged scene, or inherited bias.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Grounding text in images may still leave both modalities ungrounded in the world.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The visual-poetry examples imply that technical substrate determines the ontology of the work.
rows.full: The visual-poetry examples imply that technical substrate determines the ontology of the work. But reception also matters: viewers may encounter the final image without knowing whether it came from a typewriter, script, or neural model. Media specificity and perceptual experience do not always align.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The visual-poetry examples imply that technical substrate determines the ontology of the work.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The paper defines AGI through broad reasoning, planning, and learning from experience, then calls GPT-4 an early AGI while acknowledging that planning is substantially deficient and continuous learning is absent.
rows.full: The paper defines AGI through broad reasoning, planning, and learning from experience, then calls GPT-4 an early AGI while acknowledging that planning is substantially deficient and continuous learning is absent. The conclusion depends on treating breadth as sufficient evidence despite missing parts of the stated definition.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The paper defines AGI through broad reasoning, planning, and learning from experience, then calls GPT-4 an early AGI while acknowledging that planning is substantially deficient and continuous learning is absent.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The authors reject conventional benchmarks because training-data contamination is unknowable, but replace them with hand-selected demonstrations whose novelty, difficulty, and representativeness are also difficult to establish.
rows.full: The authors reject conventional benchmarks because training-data contamination is unknowable, but replace them with hand-selected demonstrations whose novelty, difficulty, and representativeness are also difficult to establish.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The authors reject conventional benchmarks because training-data contamination is unknowable, but replace them with hand-selected demonstrations whose novelty, difficulty, and representativeness are also difficult to establish.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Human creativity is presented as an evaluation strength, yet it introduces researcher discretion at every stage: which prompts are attempted, which outputs are shown, how often a task is retried, which comparison seems fair, and what counts as impressive.
rows.full: Human creativity is presented as an evaluation strength, yet it introduces researcher discretion at every stage: which prompts are attempted, which outputs are shown, how often a task is retried, which comparison seems fair, and what counts as impressive.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Human creativity is presented as an evaluation strength, yet it introduces researcher discretion at every stage: which prompts are attempted, which outputs are shown, how often a task is retried, which comparison seems fair, and what counts as impressive.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The model was changing during the investigation.
rows.full: The model was changing during the investigation. The improving TikZ unicorn is offered as evidence of development, but a moving target prevents clean reproduction, stable measurement, and attribution of changes to particular interventions.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The model was changing during the investigation.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The paper sometimes treats successful output as evidence of internal understanding while explaining failures through architectural constraints, prompting, or evaluation design.
rows.full: The paper sometimes treats successful output as evidence of internal understanding while explaining failures through architectural constraints, prompting, or evaluation design. Success is readily attributed to the model; failure is more often distributed across the surrounding system.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The paper sometimes treats successful output as evidence of internal understanding while explaining failures through architectural constraints, prompting, or evaluation design.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: GPT-4 is occasionally asked to evaluate its own work against another model’s output.
rows.full: GPT-4 is occasionally asked to evaluate its own work against another model’s output. This introduces circularity, possible self-preference, and dependence on the same model whose judgment is under investigation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: GPT-4 is occasionally asked to evaluate its own work against another model’s output.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The paper calls generated visual code evidence that a text-only system can “see.” More precisely, it demonstrates that the model can manipulate textual representations whose execution produces recognizable geometry.
rows.full: The paper calls generated visual code evidence that a text-only system can “see.” More precisely, it demonstrates that the model can manipulate textual representations whose execution produces recognizable geometry. That is significant, but it is not equivalent to perceptual contact with images.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The paper calls generated visual code evidence that a text-only system can “see.” More precisely, it demonstrates that the model can manipulate textual representations whose execution produces recognizable geometry.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The claim of near-human performance compares a persistent, rapidly sampled model against loosely specified humans.
rows.full: The claim of near-human performance compares a persistent, rapidly sampled model against loosely specified humans. Human expertise, preparation, time, access to tools, incentives, and evaluation conditions are not consistently matched.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The claim of near-human performance compares a persistent, rapidly sampled model against loosely specified humans.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Geertz rejects culture as a causal force and defines it as context, yet contexts influence what actions are possible, intelligible, rewarded, or punished.
rows.full: Geertz rejects culture as a causal force and defines it as context, yet contexts influence what actions are possible, intelligible, rewarded, or punished. The distinction between explanation by meaning and explanation by cause is less clean than the argument suggests.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Geertz rejects culture as a causal force and defines it as context, yet contexts influence what actions are possible, intelligible, rewarded, or punished.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Meaning is declared public, but intention still matters centrally to distinguishing wink, parody, rehearsal, and fraud.
rows.full: Meaning is declared public, but intention still matters centrally to distinguishing wink, parody, rehearsal, and fraud. The account resists mentalism while repeatedly relying on inferred purposes that cannot be directly observed.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Meaning is declared public, but intention still matters centrally to distinguishing wink, parody, rehearsal, and fraud.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The ethnographer is warned against pretending to become native, yet must formulate accounts in terms of actors’ own interpretations.
rows.full: The ethnographer is warned against pretending to become native, yet must formulate accounts in terms of actors’ own interpretations. The method requires proximity to another point of view while denying that full access is possible.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The ethnographer is warned against pretending to become native, yet must formulate accounts in terms of actors’ own interpretations.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Geertz emphasizes the fashioned, fictional character of ethnographic writing but defends its claim to knowledge through interpretive power.
rows.full: Geertz emphasizes the fashioned, fictional character of ethnographic writing but defends its claim to knowledge through interpretive power. The criteria separating disciplined construction from persuasive invention remain under-specified.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Geertz emphasizes the fashioned, fictional character of ethnographic writing but defends its claim to knowledge through interpretive power.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The article criticizes formalism and the dream of an “ethnographic algorithm,” yet it offers reusable conceptual structures-thin versus thick description, inscription, public meaning, symbolic action-that can themselves harden into a new formula.
rows.full: The article criticizes formalism and the dream of an “ethnographic algorithm,” yet it offers reusable conceptual structures-thin versus thick description, inscription, public meaning, symbolic action-that can themselves harden into a new formula.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article criticizes formalism and the dream of an “ethnographic algorithm,” yet it offers reusable conceptual structures-thin versus thick description, inscription, public meaning, symbolic action-that can themselves harden into a new formula.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Interpretation must stay close to specific events, but it is also expected to speak to large questions.
rows.full: Interpretation must stay close to specific events, but it is also expected to speak to large questions. “Small facts speak to large issues because they are made to” accurately names the inferential leap without fully regulating it.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Interpretation must stay close to specific events, but it is also expected to speak to large questions.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Theory is said to generalize within cases rather than across them, yet concepts such as culture, symbol, discourse, and meaning are repeatedly moved between very different cases.
rows.full: Theory is said to generalize within cases rather than across them, yet concepts such as culture, symbol, discourse, and meaning are repeatedly moved between very different cases. The distinction protects particularity but cannot eliminate cross-case abstraction.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Theory is said to generalize within cases rather than across them, yet concepts such as culture, symbol, discourse, and meaning are repeatedly moved between very different cases.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Geertz treats actor-oriented interpretation as essential, but actors themselves disagree, deceive, misunderstand, and inhabit unequal positions.
rows.full: Geertz treats actor-oriented interpretation as essential, but actors themselves disagree, deceive, misunderstand, and inhabit unequal positions. There may be no singular “actor’s point of view” to recover.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Geertz treats actor-oriented interpretation as essential, but actors themselves disagree, deceive, misunderstand, and inhabit unequal positions.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The account treats public meaning as accessible through interpretation while also insisting that analysis is intrinsically incomplete.
rows.full: The account treats public meaning as accessible through interpretation while also insisting that analysis is intrinsically incomplete. This leaves appraisal dependent partly on judgment, disciplinary authority, and rhetorical force.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The account treats public meaning as accessible through interpretation while also insisting that analysis is intrinsically incomplete.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: A simulator normally supports interventions: an agent takes an action and the environment returns a consequential next state.
rows.full: A simulator normally supports interventions: an agent takes an action and the environment returns a consequential next state. Most evidence presented here concerns unconditional or prompt-conditioned video generation, not systematic action-conditioned dynamics.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A simulator normally supports interventions: an agent takes an action and the environment returns a consequential next state.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Visual plausibility is not causal fidelity.
rows.full: Visual plausibility is not causal fidelity. A video may look physically coherent while relying on incorrect hidden mechanics. Conversely, a physically accurate simulation may look visually crude.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Visual plausibility is not causal fidelity.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The report cites object permanence and state persistence as emergent capabilities while acknowledging spontaneous object appearances, incorrect eating behavior, broken physics, and long-duration incoherence.
rows.full: The report cites object permanence and state persistence as emergent capabilities while acknowledging spontaneous object appearances, incorrect eating behavior, broken physics, and long-duration incoherence. The evidence for simulation and the evidence against it arise from the same outputs.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The report cites object permanence and state persistence as emergent capabilities while acknowledging spontaneous object appearances, incorrect eating behavior, broken physics, and long-duration incoherence.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Interpolation between two videos can produce smooth transitions without representing any possible path through the world.
rows.full: Interpolation between two videos can produce smooth transitions without representing any possible path through the world. Semantic morphing may resemble temporal continuity while violating identity, causality, or material conservation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Interpolation between two videos can produce smooth transitions without representing any possible path through the world.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The Minecraft example is framed as simulation of a digital world, but visual imitation of gameplay does not establish that the model has learned the game’s rule system, inventory logic, collision mechanics, or persistent hidden state.
rows.full: The Minecraft example is framed as simulation of a digital world, but visual imitation of gameplay does not establish that the model has learned the game’s rule system, inventory logic, collision mechanics, or persistent hidden state.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The Minecraft example is framed as simulation of a digital world, but visual imitation of gameplay does not establish that the model has learned the game’s rule system, inventory logic, collision mechanics, or persistent hidden state.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The claim that 3D consistency emerges without explicit 3D inductive bias is important, but training video already contains structured projections of three-dimensional scenes.
rows.full: The claim that 3D consistency emerges without explicit 3D inductive bias is important, but training video already contains structured projections of three-dimensional scenes. The bias may be present statistically in the data rather than architecturally in the model.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The claim that 3D consistency emerges without explicit 3D inductive bias is important, but training video already contains structured projections of three-dimensional scenes.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Scaling evidence is presented qualitatively through selected outputs at different compute levels.
rows.full: Scaling evidence is presented qualitatively through selected outputs at different compute levels. Improved sample quality does not by itself establish improved causal modeling, physical accuracy, or general simulation competence.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Scaling evidence is presented qualitatively through selected outputs at different compute levels.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The report calls Sora a generalist model while withholding model architecture details, training data composition, evaluation procedures, and implementation specifics.
rows.full: The report calls Sora a generalist model while withholding model architecture details, training data composition, evaluation procedures, and implementation specifics. Generality is asserted through demonstrations that outside researchers cannot fully reproduce.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The report calls Sora a generalist model while withholding model architecture details, training data composition, evaluation procedures, and implementation specifics.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Prompt expansion improves adherence but complicates attribution.
rows.full: Prompt expansion improves adherence but complicates attribution. It becomes unclear whether a successful output reflects the video model’s understanding, the captioning model’s elaboration, hidden prompting conventions, or selective sampling.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Prompt expansion improves adherence but complicates attribution.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The page defines world models as systems that “understand” physical dynamics, but most described capabilities concern generation, prediction, curation, and post-training.
rows.full: The page defines world models as systems that “understand” physical dynamics, but most described capabilities concern generation, prediction, curation, and post-training. Successful visual continuation does not establish understanding in a strong causal sense.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The page defines world models as systems that “understand” physical dynamics, but most described capabilities concern generation, prediction, curation, and post-training.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Traditional rule-based simulators are described as precise but brittle, while learned models are presented as adaptive and general.
rows.full: Traditional rule-based simulators are described as precise but brittle, while learned models are presented as adaptive and general. Yet learned systems can be less interpretable, less constrained, and less reliable precisely in the rare conditions where physical systems require guarantees.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Traditional rule-based simulators are described as precise but brittle, while learned models are presented as adaptive and general.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The claim that autoregressive models “understand cause and effect in time” is stronger than the architecture alone supports.
rows.full: The claim that autoregressive models “understand cause and effect in time” is stronger than the architecture alone supports. Sequential prediction can learn statistical regularities without identifying causal mechanisms.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The claim that autoregressive models “understand cause and effect in time” is stronger than the architecture alone supports.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The page says diffusion models refine outputs simultaneously and provide stronger spatial consistency, but diffusion still proceeds through multiple denoising steps.
rows.full: The page says diffusion models refine outputs simultaneously and provide stronger spatial consistency, but diffusion still proceeds through multiple denoising steps. “Simultaneous” describes position-wise generation within a pass more than the absence of temporal computation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The page says diffusion models refine outputs simultaneously and provide stronger spatial consistency, but diffusion still proceeds through multiple denoising steps.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Synthetic data are presented as enabling performance on unseen situations.
rows.full: Synthetic data are presented as enabling performance on unseen situations. But synthetic environments can only vary along dimensions represented by their generators, simulators, prompts, and scenario designers. Unknown unknowns cannot be reliably generated by a model that does not represent them.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Synthetic data are presented as enabling performance on unseen situations.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: Photorealism is presented as helping close the sim-to-real gap.
rows.full: Photorealism is presented as helping close the sim-to-real gap. Visual resemblance can reduce perceptual mismatch while leaving friction, deformability, latency, sensor noise, human unpredictability, and hidden physical state incorrectly modeled.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Photorealism is presented as helping close the sim-to-real gap.

rows.label: TENSION
rows.kernel: The claim that one foundation model can generalize across embodiments underplays how strongly action possibilities depend on body geometry, sensors, actuators, control frequency, balance, energy, and failure tolerance.
rows.full: The claim that one foundation model can generalize across embodiments underplays how strongly action possibilities depend on body geometry, sensors, actuators, control frequency, balance, energy, and failure tolerance.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The claim that one foundation model can generalize across embodiments underplays how strongly action possibilities depend on body geometry, sensors, actuators, control frequency, balance, energy, and failure tolerance.

rows.label: TENSION
