rows.full: The evidence suggests intelligence is not one scalar quantity but a profile: <Domain_Breadth> <Conceptual_Composition> <Local_Reasoning> <Planning_Horizon> <Error_Recovery> <Tool_Use> <Learning_Rate> <Grounding> <Judgment> <Reliability>. A system can be extraordinary on several dimensions and structurally weak on others.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The evidence suggests intelligence is not one scalar quantity but a profile: <Domain_Breadth> <Conceptual_Composition> <Local_Reasoning> <Planning_Horizon> <Error_Recovery> <Tool_Use> <Learning_Rate> <Grounding> <Judgment> <Reliability>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The paper moves evaluation from: <Fixed_Benchmark> → <Aggregate_Score> toward: <Novel_Task> → <Interactive_Probe> → <Perturbation> → <Revision> → <Failure_Analysis>.
rows.full: The paper moves evaluation from: <Fixed_Benchmark> → <Aggregate_Score> toward: <Novel_Task> → <Interactive_Probe> → <Perturbation> → <Revision> → <Failure_Analysis>. This is closer to an oral examination than a standardized test, but it requires stronger procedural discipline than the paper supplies.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The paper moves evaluation from: <Fixed_Benchmark> → <Aggregate_Score> toward: <Novel_Task> → <Interactive_Probe> → <Perturbation> → <Revision> → <Failure_Analysis>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: If next-token prediction provides broad local competence but weak global planning, the likely path forward is not merely a larger predictor.
rows.full: If next-token prediction provides broad local competence but weak global planning, the likely path forward is not merely a larger predictor. It is a system that separates: <Generation> <Planning> <Memory> <Verification> <Tool_Execution> <World_Feedback> <Learning_From_Consequences>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: If next-token prediction provides broad local competence but weak global planning, the likely path forward is not merely a larger predictor.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The paper’s “vision” experiments show that executable text can become a visual medium.
rows.full: The paper’s “vision” experiments show that executable text can become a visual medium. GPT-4 does not cross from text into image through direct perception; it composes symbolic operations that cause images to appear. Intelligence here lies partly in controlling transformations between representational systems.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The paper’s “vision” experiments show that executable text can become a visual medium.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Calling a proprietary and inaccessible system an early AGI does institutional work.
rows.full: Calling a proprietary and inaccessible system an early AGI does institutional work. It elevates one company’s model from product to historical threshold and grants its developers unusual authority to define intelligence, risk, evaluation, and the direction of future research.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Calling a proprietary and inaccessible system an early AGI does institutional work.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Behavior does not carry its meaning on its surface.
rows.full: Behavior does not carry its meaning on its surface. To understand an act is to reconstruct the layered social conditions under which that movement, phrase, object, or omission counts as the act it is.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Behavior does not carry its meaning on its surface.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Observable_Event> → <Possible_Social_Codes> → <Actor_Intentions> → <Audience_Interpretations> → <Historical_Conditions> → <Institutional_Power> → <Competing_Frames> → <Provisional_Reading>.
rows.full: <Observable_Event> → <Possible_Social_Codes> → <Actor_Intentions> → <Audience_Interpretations> → <Historical_Conditions> → <Institutional_Power> → <Competing_Frames> → <Provisional_Reading>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Observable_Event> → <Possible_Social_Codes> → <Actor_Intentions> → <Audience_Interpretations> → <Historical_Conditions> → <Institutional_Power> → <Competing_Frames> → <Provisional_Reading>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Description and explanation cannot be cleanly separated.
rows.full: Description and explanation cannot be cleanly separated. Choosing what to record, naming the action, arranging its context, and deciding which interpretation matters are already analytic acts.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Description and explanation cannot be cleanly separated.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A model that recognizes eyelid motion has achieved thin description.
rows.full: A model that recognizes eyelid motion has achieved thin description. A model that identifies a wink requires social convention. A model that distinguishes satire, rehearsal, deception, and misrecognition requires recursive models of actors, audiences, histories, and power. The gap between perception and cultural understanding is therefore not simply a matter of adding more sensory data.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A model that recognizes eyelid motion has achieved thin description.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A world model that predicts physical state transitions can still fail to model social reality.
rows.full: A world model that predicts physical state transitions can still fail to model social reality. The same bodily action can alter alliances, obligations, threats, jokes, or legal standing depending on the interpretive frame in which it occurs.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A world model that predicts physical state transitions can still fail to model social reality.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Combining text, image, sound, and action does not by itself produce thick understanding.
rows.full: Combining text, image, sound, and action does not by itself produce thick understanding. Multimodality may enrich the observable surface while leaving the socially established code unresolved.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Combining text, image, sound, and action does not by itself produce thick understanding.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Correct classification should not be evaluated only against a label such as <wink>.
rows.full: Correct classification should not be evaluated only against a label such as <wink>. It should be tested against competing plausible interpretations and require an account of which contextual evidence changes the classification.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Correct classification should not be evaluated only against a label such as <wink>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Sora suggests that sufficiently scaled video prediction can acquire partial regularities of space, time, identity, and interaction without being explicitly programmed with objects or physical laws.
rows.full: Sora suggests that sufficiently scaled video prediction can acquire partial regularities of space, time, identity, and interaction without being explicitly programmed with objects or physical laws. But this establishes a promising representational substrate-not yet a dependable world simulator.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Sora suggests that sufficiently scaled video prediction can acquire partial regularities of space, time, identity, and interaction without being explicitly programmed with objects or physical laws.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Renderer> produces convincing appearances.
rows.full: <Renderer> produces convincing appearances. <Temporal_Renderer> preserves appearance across time. <Generative_World> supports editable and extendable scenes. <Simulator> predicts consequences of interventions. <Planner_World_Model> supports comparison among possible actions. Sora demonstrates substantial movement through the first three categories; the report extrapolates toward the fourth.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Renderer> produces convincing appearances.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Raw_Visual_Data> → <Compression_Network> → <Spacetime_Latents> → <Patch_Sequence> → <Diffusion_Transformer> → <Generated_Latents> → <Decoder> → <Visible_Video>.
rows.full: <Raw_Visual_Data> → <Compression_Network> → <Spacetime_Latents> → <Patch_Sequence> → <Diffusion_Transformer> → <Generated_Latents> → <Decoder> → <Visible_Video>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Raw_Visual_Data> → <Compression_Network> → <Spacetime_Latents> → <Patch_Sequence> → <Diffusion_Transformer> → <Generated_Latents> → <Decoder> → <Visible_Video>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <User_Prompt> → <GPT_Expansion> → <Detailed_Caption> → <Text_Conditioning> → <Latent_Denoising> → <Video>.
rows.full: <User_Prompt> → <GPT_Expansion> → <Detailed_Caption> → <Text_Conditioning> → <Latent_Denoising> → <Video>. The generated world is conditioned by a machine-authored interpretation of the request.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <User_Prompt> → <GPT_Expansion> → <Detailed_Caption> → <Text_Conditioning> → <Latent_Denoising> → <Video>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A useful model of the world must preserve different things for different purposes.
rows.full: A useful model of the world must preserve different things for different purposes. Cinema requires visual continuity. Robotics requires intervention fidelity. Science requires measurable state. Social interaction requires meaning, intention, and norm recognition. One representation may not satisfy all four.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A useful model of the world must preserve different things for different purposes.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Sora can generate increasingly thick sensory surfaces while remaining socially thin.
rows.full: Sora can generate increasingly thick sensory surfaces while remaining socially thin. It may render the movement of a wink without knowing whether the act is conspiracy, parody, rehearsal, flirtation, or neurological twitch.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Sora can generate increasingly thick sensory surfaces while remaining socially thin.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The immediate product is not necessarily a physical simulator.
rows.full: The immediate product is not necessarily a physical simulator. It is a reusable synthetic production environment for advertising, entertainment, design, prototyping, training data, and visualization. “World simulation” supplies the longer strategic horizon.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The immediate product is not necessarily a physical simulator.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: NVIDIA’s “world model” is best understood as a synthetic-experience operating system for physical AI rather than a singular internal representation of reality.
rows.full: NVIDIA’s “world model” is best understood as a synthetic-experience operating system for physical AI rather than a singular internal representation of reality.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: NVIDIA’s “world model” is best understood as a synthetic-experience operating system for physical AI rather than a singular internal representation of reality.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Real_World_Data> → <Curation> → <Captioning_And_Search> → <Tokenization> → <World_Foundation_Model> → <Synthetic_Scenarios> → <Embodiment_Specific_Post_Training> → <Policy_Learning> → <Simulation_Validation> → <Physical…
rows.full: <Real_World_Data> → <Curation> → <Captioning_And_Search> → <Tokenization> → <World_Foundation_Model> → <Synthetic_Scenarios> → <Embodiment_Specific_Post_Training> → <Policy_Learning> → <Simulation_Validation> → <Physical_Deployment> → <Failure_Data> → <Model_Update>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Real_World_Data> → <Curation> → <Captioning_And_Search> → <Tokenization> → <World_Foundation_Model> → <Synthetic_Scenarios> → <Embodiment_Specific_Post_Training> → <Policy_Learning> → <Simulation_Validation> → <Physical…

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Renderer> creates plausible appearances.
rows.full: <Renderer> creates plausible appearances. <Predictor> estimates future observations. <Simulator> applies intervention-conditioned state transitions. <Planner_Model> evaluates possible actions. <Policy> selects actions. <Controller> executes them through a specific body. <Verifier> determines whether predicted and actual consequences agree. The page frequently treats these as one continuum, but their failure modes differ.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Renderer> creates plausible appearances.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The system aims to model physical state richly while risking social thinness.
rows.full: The system aims to model physical state richly while risking social thinness. It may identify a person falling, raising a hand, entering a restricted zone, or moving unusually without knowing whether the act is accident, ritual, protest, parody, disability, or ordinary local practice.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The system aims to model physical state richly while risking social thinness.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: OpenAI’s Sora report moves from video generation toward simulation through emergent continuity.
rows.full: OpenAI’s Sora report moves from video generation toward simulation through emergent continuity. NVIDIA moves from simulation infrastructure toward video generation as a means of scaling physical training. One begins with rendering and claims a path to worlds; the other begins with physical AI and absorbs generation into the training stack.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: OpenAI’s Sora report moves from video generation toward simulation through emergent continuity.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The provider of the world foundation model controls the representational substrate through which downstream robots learn what counts as an object, hazard, normal trajectory, plausible human behavior, and successful action.
rows.full: The provider of the world foundation model controls the representational substrate through which downstream robots learn what counts as an object, hazard, normal trajectory, plausible human behavior, and successful action.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The provider of the world foundation model controls the representational substrate through which downstream robots learn what counts as an object, hazard, normal trajectory, plausible human behavior, and successful action.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Genie 3 turns generated media into a responsive environment.
rows.full: Genie 3 turns generated media into a responsive environment. Its importance lies not primarily in better-looking video, but in making synthetic visual worlds answer actions quickly enough to become places of rehearsal.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Genie 3 turns generated media into a responsive environment.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <World_Description> + <Prior_Frames> + <Action_History> + <Current_User_Input> → <Next_Visual_State> → <New_Action> → <Extended_Trajectory>.
rows.full: <World_Description> + <Prior_Frames> + <Action_History> + <Current_User_Input> → <Next_Visual_State> → <New_Action> → <Extended_Trajectory>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <World_Description> + <Prior_Frames> + <Action_History> + <Current_User_Input> → <Next_Visual_State> → <New_Action> → <Extended_Trajectory>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Navigation_Control> selects movement inside the current world.
rows.full: <Navigation_Control> selects movement inside the current world. <World_Event_Control> alters the world’s conditions through language. This resembles a game in which the player and the dungeon master operate through different interfaces.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Navigation_Control> selects movement inside the current world.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Sora> demonstrates long-form visual generation and partial physical continuity.
rows.full: <Sora> demonstrates long-form visual generation and partial physical continuity. <Genie_3> adds real-time action conditioning and short-term navigable persistence. The transition is: <Video_Of_A_World> → <World_That_Responds>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Sora> demonstrates long-form visual generation and partial physical continuity.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The earlier World Models architecture learned a compact simulator and trained a small controller inside it.
rows.full: The earlier World Models architecture learned a compact simulator and trained a small controller inside it. Genie 3 instead generates the perceptual environment itself in real time and allows a separate agent to act through that stream. The simulator is no longer merely latent; it becomes the agent’s visible sensorium.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The earlier World Models architecture learned a compact simulator and trained a small controller inside it.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Genie 3 can generate rich physical surroundings but remains culturally thin.
rows.full: Genie 3 can generate rich physical surroundings but remains culturally thin. It may render a Victorian street, palace, festival, or ancient city without modeling the public codes through which actions there acquire social meaning.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Genie 3 can generate rich physical surroundings but remains culturally thin.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Whoever designs the world generator also designs the agent’s formative experience.
rows.full: Whoever designs the world generator also designs the agent’s formative experience. Dataset composition, prompt language, visual conventions, omitted hazards, and preferred scenarios become part of the future agent’s practical common sense.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Whoever designs the world generator also designs the agent’s formative experience.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A robotic world model should be understood as a counterfactual action instrument: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Action_Selection> → <Physical_Execution> → <Observed_Discrepancy> → <Model_Revision>.
rows.full: A robotic world model should be understood as a counterfactual action instrument: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Action_Selection> → <Physical_Execution> → <Observed_Discrepancy> → <Model_Revision>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A robotic world model should be understood as a counterfactual action instrument: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Action_Selection> → <Physical_Execution> → <Observed_Discrepancy> → <Model_Revision>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Renderer> creates plausible future appearances.
rows.full: <Renderer> creates plausible future appearances. <Forecaster> predicts likely future observations. <Controllable_Model> distinguishes futures under different actions. <Simulator> sustains closed-loop interaction. <Evaluator> ranks predicted consequences. <Policy> selects actions. <Controller> realizes those actions through a particular body. The survey shows these roles increasingly merging, but they should remain separately testable.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Renderer> creates plausible future appearances.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The central metric should be <action-counterfactual fidelity>: Given the same initial state, does changing only the action produce the correctly different consequence?
rows.full: The central metric should be <action-counterfactual fidelity>: Given the same initial state, does changing only the action produce the correctly different consequence? Visual realism is secondary when the system will control machinery.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The central metric should be <action-counterfactual fidelity>: Given the same initial state, does changing only the action produce the correctly different consequence?

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The field is moving through: <External_World_Model> → <World_Model_As_Policy_Input> → <Shared_Predictive_Control_Backbone> → <Latent_Internalized_World_Model> → <Policy_And_Simulator_Co_Evolution>.
rows.full: The field is moving through: <External_World_Model> → <World_Model_As_Policy_Input> → <Shared_Predictive_Control_Backbone> → <Latent_Internalized_World_Model> → <Policy_And_Simulator_Co_Evolution>. Each step improves integration while making independent verification more difficult.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The field is moving through: <External_World_Model> → <World_Model_As_Policy_Input> → <Shared_Predictive_Control_Backbone> → <Latent_Internalized_World_Model> → <Policy_And_Simulator_Co_Evolution>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Future robotics datasets need more than successful demonstrations.
rows.full: Future robotics datasets need more than successful demonstrations. They require: <Failures> <Near_Misses> <Recovery_Trajectories> <Alternative_Actions> <Contact_And_Force> <Delayed_Consequences> <Human_Interruption> <Environment_Change>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Future robotics datasets need more than successful demonstrations.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: These systems model physical transitions more readily than social meaning.
rows.full: These systems model physical transitions more readily than social meaning. A robot may predict that a raised hand precedes a movement while failing to distinguish greeting, warning, command, surrender, ritual, disability, or joke. Physical foresight can remain culturally thin.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: These systems model physical transitions more readily than social meaning.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Once a world model trains, evaluates, and filters robot behavior, it becomes a constitutional layer.
rows.full: Once a world model trains, evaluates, and filters robot behavior, it becomes a constitutional layer. It determines which consequences are imaginable, which risks are visible, and which actions appear rational before the machine enters the world.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Once a world model trains, evaluates, and filters robot behavior, it becomes a constitutional layer.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: WorldDreamer proposes that a world model need not begin by predicting the next frame.
rows.full: WorldDreamer proposes that a world model need not begin by predicting the next frame. It can instead learn a distribution over complete visual events and generate particular futures by selectively revealing, masking, and conditioning parts of that event.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: WorldDreamer proposes that a world model need not begin by predicting the next frame.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Images_And_Videos> → <VQGAN_Tokenization> → <Masked_Visual_Sequence> + <Text_Embedding> + <Action_Embedding> → <Spatial_Temporal_Patchwise_Transformer> → <Predicted_Tokens> → <Visual_Decoder> → <Generated_Or_Edited_Video>.
rows.full: <Images_And_Videos> → <VQGAN_Tokenization> → <Masked_Visual_Sequence> + <Text_Embedding> + <Action_Embedding> → <Spatial_Temporal_Patchwise_Transformer> → <Predicted_Tokens> → <Visual_Decoder> → <Generated_Or_Edited_Video>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Images_And_Videos> → <VQGAN_Tokenization> → <Masked_Visual_Sequence> + <Text_Embedding> + <Action_Embedding> → <Spatial_Temporal_Patchwise_Transformer> → <Predicted_Tokens> → <Visual_Decoder> → <Generated_Or_Edited_Video>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Text_To_Video> all visual tokens are masked.
rows.full: <Text_To_Video> all visual tokens are masked. <Image_To_Video> the first frame is visible and later frames are masked. <Video_Inpainting> a spatial region is masked through time. <Video_Stylization> tokens are partially masked and reconstructed under a style prompt. <Action_To_Video> future visual tokens are predicted under control signals.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Text_To_Video> all visual tokens are masked.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Visual_Completion_Model> reconstructs masked appearances.
rows.full: <Visual_Completion_Model> reconstructs masked appearances. <Video_Generator> produces plausible moving imagery. <Action_Conditioned_Predictor> distinguishes futures under controls. <World_Simulator> supports repeated closed-loop intervention. <Planner_Model> evaluates possible actions by their predicted consequences. WorldDreamer strongly demonstrates the first two and begins to demonstrate the third.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Visual_Completion_Model> reconstructs masked appearances.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The prompt does not merely describe a video.
rows.full: The prompt does not merely describe a video. It participates causally in filling missing visual tokens. Language becomes an operator over a partially specified visual world: <Description> → <Latent_Constraint> → <Token_Selection> → <Moving_Image>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The prompt does not merely describe a video.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: WorldDreamer and Sora both create common representations for heterogeneous visual media, but their generative logics differ: <WorldDreamer> reconstructs discrete masked tokens in parallel.
rows.full: WorldDreamer and Sora both create common representations for heterogeneous visual media, but their generative logics differ: <WorldDreamer> reconstructs discrete masked tokens in parallel. <Sora> denoises continuous spacetime latent patches. Both turn visual media into transformer-processable units and infer world understanding from scalable video generation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: WorldDreamer and Sora both create common representations for heterogeneous visual media, but their generative logics differ: <WorldDreamer> reconstructs discrete masked tokens in parallel.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: WorldDreamer can generate action-conditioned driving videos, but Genie attempts continuous real-time interaction.
rows.full: WorldDreamer can generate action-conditioned driving videos, but Genie attempts continuous real-time interaction. The difference is between: <Action_Specified_Clip> and <Action_Response_Loop>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: WorldDreamer can generate action-conditioned driving videos, but Genie attempts continuous real-time interaction.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Masked world modeling may be especially useful where multiple forms of conditioning, editing, reconstruction, and counterfactual completion must share one model.
rows.full: Masked world modeling may be especially useful where multiple forms of conditioning, editing, reconstruction, and counterfactual completion must share one model. It is less obviously sufficient for irreversible closed-loop control where only past evidence is available.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Masked world modeling may be especially useful where multiple forms of conditioning, editing, reconstruction, and counterfactual completion must share one model.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The main research question is not whether robots need language or world models.
rows.full: The main research question is not whether robots need language or world models. It is which representational path most efficiently converts human intent into physically valid change.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The main research question is not whether robots need language or world models.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <VLA_Path> <Language_And_Image_Understanding> → <Action_Grounding> → <Control>.
rows.full: <VLA_Path> <Language_And_Image_Understanding> → <Action_Grounding> → <Control>. <WAM_Path> <Language_Conditioned_Dynamics> → <Future_State_Or_Latent_Plan> → <Action_Recovery> → <Control>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <VLA_Path> <Language_And_Image_Understanding> → <Action_Grounding> → <Control>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Understanding_Expert> interprets language, objects, relations, and social context.
rows.full: <Understanding_Expert> interprets language, objects, relations, and social context. <Predictive_Expert> models possible future states and action consequences. <Action_Expert> translates selected futures into embodiment-specific control. <Verifier> checks feasibility, uncertainty, safety, and real-world discrepancy. <Memory> preserves task state and consequences across long horizons.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Understanding_Expert> interprets language, objects, relations, and social context.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The important boundary is where imagination stops: <Full_Video_At_Inference> <Partial_Latent_Rollout> <Single_Visual_Subgoal> <Compact_Future_Embedding> <Training_Only_Predictive_Objective>.
rows.full: The important boundary is where imagination stops: <Full_Video_At_Inference> <Partial_Latent_Rollout> <Single_Visual_Subgoal> <Compact_Future_Embedding> <Training_Only_Predictive_Objective>. These may provide similar benefits at radically different latency and auditability costs.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The important boundary is where imagination stops: <Full_Video_At_Inference> <Partial_Latent_Rollout> <Single_Visual_Subgoal> <Compact_Future_Embedding> <Training_Only_Predictive_Objective>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: WAMs exchange robot-data scarcity for video-compute abundance.
rows.full: WAMs exchange robot-data scarcity for video-compute abundance. They may require fewer physical demonstrations while inheriting enormous costs in video collection, captioning, preprocessing, pretraining, storage, and high-latency inference.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: WAMs exchange robot-data scarcity for video-compute abundance.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A clean comparison must hold constant: <Robot_Data> <Embodiment> <Parameter_Count> <Pretraining_Compute> <Action_Head> <Inference_Budget> <Test_Distribution>.
rows.full: A clean comparison must hold constant: <Robot_Data> <Embodiment> <Parameter_Count> <Pretraining_Compute> <Action_Head> <Inference_Budget> <Test_Distribution>. Without this, “VLA versus WAM” remains partly a contest between entire industrial stacks.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A clean comparison must hold constant: <Robot_Data> <Embodiment> <Parameter_Count> <Pretraining_Compute> <Action_Head> <Inference_Budget> <Test_Distribution>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Video pretraining may teach what an action visually resembles without teaching what the action socially means.
rows.full: Video pretraining may teach what an action visually resembles without teaching what the action socially means. A robot may model the movement required to hand over an object while missing whether the exchange is invitation, obligation, refusal, theft, ceremony, or joke.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Video pretraining may teach what an action visually resembles without teaching what the action socially means.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Knowlton’s system establishes animation as a programmable relation among: <Visual_State> <Spatial_Operation> <Temporal_Exposure> <Camera_Configuration> <Storage> <Transformation> <Output_Medium>.
rows.full: Knowlton’s system establishes animation as a programmable relation among: <Visual_State> <Spatial_Operation> <Temporal_Exposure> <Camera_Configuration> <Storage> <Transformation> <Output_Medium>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Knowlton’s system establishes animation as a programmable relation among: <Visual_State> <Spatial_Operation> <Temporal_Exposure> <Camera_Configuration> <Storage> <Transformation> <Output_Medium>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Program> → <Surface_State> → <Scanner_Operations> → <Movie_Macros> → <Camera_Call> → <Frame_Duration_Record> → <Microfilm_Master> → <Optical_Printing> → <Final_Film>.
rows.full: <Program> → <Surface_State> → <Scanner_Operations> → <Movie_Macros> → <Camera_Call> → <Frame_Duration_Record> → <Microfilm_Master> → <Optical_Printing> → <Final_Film>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Program> → <Surface_State> → <Scanner_Operations> → <Movie_Macros> → <Camera_Call> → <Frame_Duration_Record> → <Microfilm_Master> → <Optical_Printing> → <Final_Film>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Scanner_Language> provides local reading, writing, movement, tests, and control flow.
rows.full: <Scanner_Language> provides local reading, writing, movement, tests, and control flow. <Movie_Language> provides image-level drafting, compositing, transformation, storage, and temporal operations. <User_Macros> provide project-specific cinematic concepts built from the lower layers.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Scanner_Language> provides local reading, writing, movement, tests, and control flow.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Animation occurs when a transformation exposes its intermediate states to the camera.
rows.full: Animation occurs when a transformation exposes its intermediate states to the camera. <Hidden_Intermediate_States> produce an instantaneous edit. <Recorded_Intermediate_States> produce visible movement. The difference between editing and animation is therefore not the operation itself, but whether its process is inscribed as time.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Animation occurs when a transformation exposes its intermediate states to the camera.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The same machine infrastructure produces text, diagrams, and moving images.
rows.full: The same machine infrastructure produces text, diagrams, and moving images. Characters are simultaneously: <Instructions_In_Code> <Symbols_In_Memory> <Glyphs_On_Film> <Grey_Value_Carriers>. Text and image are not separate media here; text technology is repurposed as a visual substrate.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The same machine infrastructure produces text, diagrams, and moving images.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The system contains a minimal executable world: <Surface> defines space.
rows.full: The system contains a minimal executable world: <Surface> defines space. <Digit> defines local state. <Scanner> defines situated agency. <Test> defines perception. <Operation> defines action. <Camera> defines observation. <Frame_Count> defines time. It is not a predictive world model, but it is an explicit operational world in which visual agents alter state according to rules.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The system contains a minimal executable world: <Surface> defines space.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The enduring design principle is that a film language should expose a small number of composable visual verbs while allowing users to define higher-order cinematic operations.
rows.full: The enduring design principle is that a film language should expose a small number of composable visual verbs while allowing users to define higher-order cinematic operations. The system’s power lies less in any single command than in the ability to compile new verbs from primitive ones.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The enduring design principle is that a film language should expose a small number of composable visual verbs while allowing users to define higher-order cinematic operations.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Cybernetic cinema is not defined by synthetic imagery.
rows.full: Cybernetic cinema is not defined by synthetic imagery. It is cinema in which perception and production form a recursive system: <Specification> → <Generation> → <Display> → <Perception> → <Evaluation> → <Modification> → <Regeneration>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Cybernetic cinema is not defined by synthetic imagery.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Photographic_Cinema> records events staged before a camera.
rows.full: <Photographic_Cinema> records events staged before a camera. <Computer_Animation> constructs frames through programmed operations. <Cybernetic_Cinema> allows generated results to modify the process that generates subsequent results. <Generative_World_Cinema> constructs environments that continue responding to viewers, agents, and prompts.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Photographic_Cinema> records events staged before a camera.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The strongest creative software should behave like Whitney’s piano: <Stable_Operational_Grammar> + <Deep_Parameter_Space> + <Immediate_Perceptual_Feedback> + <Repeatable_Performance> + <Long_Term_Mastery>.
rows.full: The strongest creative software should behave like Whitney’s piano: <Stable_Operational_Grammar> + <Deep_Parameter_Space> + <Immediate_Perceptual_Feedback> + <Repeatable_Performance> + <Long_Term_Mastery>. A system that produces an impressive result but cannot be learned, replayed, varied, or performed is not yet an instrument.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The strongest creative software should behave like Whitney’s piano: <Stable_Operational_Grammar> + <Deep_Parameter_Space> + <Immediate_Perceptual_Feedback> + <Repeatable_Performance> + <Long_Term_Mastery>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Cinema does not simply contain time.
rows.full: Cinema does not simply contain time. It shapes time by coordinating: <Recurrence> <Interval> <Accumulation> <Phase> <Counterpoint> <Acceleration> <Resolution>. Whitney’s “image of time” is therefore closer to musical composition than to the animation of illustrated objects.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Cinema does not simply contain time.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A generative world model becomes cybernetic cinema only when its outputs enter an ongoing loop of action and response.
rows.full: A generative world model becomes cybernetic cinema only when its outputs enter an ongoing loop of action and response. A text-to-video clip is generated cinema. A persistent environment that changes under intervention is cybernetic cinema.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A generative world model becomes cybernetic cinema only when its outputs enter an ongoing loop of action and response.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Knowlton supplies the operational verbs; Whitney supplies the conception of the program as a performable instrument; Youngblood supplies the feedback-loop ontology.
rows.full: Knowlton supplies the operational verbs; Whitney supplies the conception of the program as a performable instrument; Youngblood supplies the feedback-loop ontology. Together: <Primitive_Visual_Operations> → <Reusable_Cinematic_Instrument> → <Live_Composition_Of_Time>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Knowlton supplies the operational verbs; Whitney supplies the conception of the program as a performable instrument; Youngblood supplies the feedback-loop ontology.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Authorship shifts from manually determining every frame toward designing: <Rules> <Parameter_Ranges> <Feedback_Channels> <Selection_Criteria> <Temporal_Organizations> <Conditions_For_Surprise>.
rows.full: Authorship shifts from manually determining every frame toward designing: <Rules> <Parameter_Ranges> <Feedback_Channels> <Selection_Criteria> <Temporal_Organizations> <Conditions_For_Surprise>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Authorship shifts from manually determining every frame toward designing: <Rules> <Parameter_Ranges> <Feedback_Channels> <Selection_Criteria> <Temporal_Organizations> <Conditions_For_Surprise>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The fundamental unit of motion design is not the moving object but the changing relation among elements.
rows.full: The fundamental unit of motion design is not the moving object but the changing relation among elements. <Motion> becomes <Pattern> when differences in rate, direction, phase, and recurrence create expectations and perceptible resolutions.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The fundamental unit of motion design is not the moving object but the changing relation among elements.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Element_Set> → <Differential_Rates> → <Phase_Divergence> → <Pattern_Destabilization> → <Tension> → <Resonant_Alignment> → <Resolution> → <Renewed_Divergence>.
rows.full: <Element_Set> → <Differential_Rates> → <Phase_Divergence> → <Pattern_Destabilization> → <Tension> → <Resonant_Alignment> → <Resolution> → <Renewed_Divergence>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Element_Set> → <Differential_Rates> → <Phase_Divergence> → <Pattern_Destabilization> → <Tension> → <Resonant_Alignment> → <Resolution> → <Renewed_Divergence>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Visual harmony is not simultaneous prettiness or symmetry.
rows.full: Visual harmony is not simultaneous prettiness or symmetry. It is the organization of multiple periodic processes so that their convergences and divergences remain perceptually consequential across time.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Visual harmony is not simultaneous prettiness or symmetry.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A visual temporal language would be built from verbs such as: [accelerate] [delay] [phase] [converge] [diverge] [repeat] [suspend] [invert] [resolve] [interrupt].
rows.full: A visual temporal language would be built from verbs such as: [accelerate] [delay] [phase] [converge] [diverge] [repeat] [suspend] [invert] [resolve] [interrupt]. Shapes become temporary carriers of these operations.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A visual temporal language would be built from verbs such as: [accelerate] [delay] [phase] [converge] [diverge] [repeat] [suspend] [invert] [resolve] [interrupt].

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A shot should not be treated merely as an image held for a duration.
rows.full: A shot should not be treated merely as an image held for a duration. It is a controlled evolution of relations whose successive states create expectation. <Shot> = <Initial_Force_Field> + <Transformation_Rules> + <Temporal_Curve> + <Resolution_Condition>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A shot should not be treated merely as an image held for a duration.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Knowlton supplies explicit operations for changing visual surfaces.
rows.full: Knowlton supplies explicit operations for changing visual surfaces. Whitney supplies a compositional theory governing when and why those changes should occur. Knowlton asks: <What_Can_The_Image_Do?> Whitney asks: <How_Should_Those_Operations_Pattern_Time?>
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Knowlton supplies explicit operations for changing visual surfaces.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Youngblood defines cybernetic cinema through feedback between artist and machine.
rows.full: Youngblood defines cybernetic cinema through feedback between artist and machine. Whitney specifies the internal aesthetic problem that the feedback loop must solve: not merely generating variation, but distinguishing motion that accumulates meaning from motion that remains inert.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Youngblood defines cybernetic cinema through feedback between artist and machine.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A generative world can exhibit enormous activity without meaningful temporal structure.
rows.full: A generative world can exhibit enormous activity without meaningful temporal structure. For cinematic use, world simulation requires a second layer that composes state changes into tension, expectation, rhythm, and resolution.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A generative world can exhibit enormous activity without meaningful temporal structure.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A BEFLIX film language should not stop at commands for drawing and shifting.
rows.full: A BEFLIX film language should not stop at commands for drawing and shifting. It needs compositional relations among operations: <Rate_Ratio> <Phase_Offset> <Resonance_Point> <Tension_Curve> <Metric_Wave> <Resolution_Event>. These should be first-class temporal parameters rather than manually inferred from frame counts.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A BEFLIX film language should not stop at commands for drawing and shifting.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The human being is not: <Biological_Organism> + <Cultural_Overlay>.
rows.full: The human being is not: <Biological_Organism> + <Cultural_Overlay>. The stronger model is: <Biological_Capacity> ↔ <Symbolic_Environment> ↔ <Social_Practice> ↔ <Neural_Development> ↔ <Individual_Performance>. Each element helps produce and constrain the others.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The human being is not: <Biological_Organism> + <Cultural_Overlay>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Open_Ended_Capacities> → <Inherited_Symbolic_Programs> → <Situated_Practice> → <Habituated_Perception_And_Emotion> → <Particular_Competence> → <Recognizable_Personhood>.
rows.full: <Open_Ended_Capacities> → <Inherited_Symbolic_Programs> → <Situated_Practice> → <Habituated_Perception_And_Emotion> → <Particular_Competence> → <Recognizable_Personhood>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Open_Ended_Capacities> → <Inherited_Symbolic_Programs> → <Situated_Practice> → <Habituated_Perception_And_Emotion> → <Particular_Competence> → <Recognizable_Personhood>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Culture provides: <Categories> for identifying what exists.
rows.full: Culture provides: <Categories> for identifying what exists. <Rules> for determining what conduct counts as appropriate. <Scripts> for sequencing action. <Values> for ranking outcomes. <Symbols> for storing and transmitting orientation. <Sanctions> for enforcing interpretations. <Practices> through which the system is reproduced or altered.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Culture provides: <Categories> for identifying what exists.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: An AI system trained only on generic behavior classes risks reproducing the “fake universal” problem.
rows.full: An AI system trained only on generic behavior classes risks reproducing the “fake universal” problem. Categories such as: <Emotion> <Risk> <Consent> <Politeness> <Threat> <Family> <Religious_Behavior> may become so abstract that they lose local meaning, or so culturally specific that one group’s norms are falsely presented as universal.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: An AI system trained only on generic behavior classes risks reproducing the “fake universal” problem.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A physical world model predicts how objects move.
rows.full: A physical world model predicts how objects move. A cultural world model also predicts: <What_The_Action_Counts_As> <Who_Is_Authorized_To_Perform_It> <What_Obligation_It_Creates> <Which_Response_Is_Expected> <How_The_Event_Will_Be_Remembered>. Human environments are governed by symbolic state transitions as well as physical ones.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A physical world model predicts how objects move.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Geertz complicates the idea that external programs merely control a preexisting human operator.
rows.full: Geertz complicates the idea that external programs merely control a preexisting human operator. The operator and program co-develop. Human beings build symbolic systems, submit to them, modify them, and are biologically and psychologically formed through that recursive relation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Geertz complicates the idea that external programs merely control a preexisting human operator.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A system intended to support human action should not assume one context-free user.
rows.full: A system intended to support human action should not assume one context-free user. It must represent how capacities are transformed into performances by: <Language> <Norms> <Roles> <Institutions> <Histories> <Embodiments> <Local_Interpretations>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A system intended to support human action should not assume one context-free user.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A picture should be understood as an operational relation: <Model_Elements> + <Correlation_Rules> + <Transformation_Procedures> + <Preserved_Invariants> → <Possible_Consequences> → <Comparison_With_Reality>.
rows.full: A picture should be understood as an operational relation: <Model_Elements> + <Correlation_Rules> + <Transformation_Procedures> + <Preserved_Invariants> → <Possible_Consequences> → <Comparison_With_Reality>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A picture should be understood as an operational relation: <Model_Elements> + <Correlation_Rules> + <Transformation_Procedures> + <Preserved_Invariants> → <Possible_Consequences> → <Comparison_With_Reality>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: To picture a situation is not to resemble it globally.
rows.full: To picture a situation is not to resemble it globally. It is to construct a system in which selected transformations correspond to selected transformations in the target.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: To picture a situation is not to resemble it globally.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Question_Of_Interest> → <Relevant_Quantities> → <Similarity_Criteria> → <Model_Construction> → <Manipulation> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Measurement> → <Judgment_Of_Fit>.
rows.full: <Question_Of_Interest> → <Relevant_Quantities> → <Similarity_Criteria> → <Model_Construction> → <Manipulation> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Measurement> → <Judgment_Of_Fit>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Question_Of_Interest> → <Relevant_Quantities> → <Similarity_Criteria> → <Model_Construction> → <Manipulation> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Measurement> → <Judgment_Of_Fit>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Logical form is less like a hidden visual skeleton and more like a preserved transformation structure…
rows.full: Logical form is less like a hidden visual skeleton and more like a preserved transformation structure: <Representation_A> → [lawful translation] → <Representation_B> while maintaining the relations necessary for the same event, operation, or consequence to remain identifiable.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Logical form is less like a hidden visual skeleton and more like a preserved transformation structure…

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The accident model contains at least three coupled models: <Physical_Model> what trajectories and impacts are possible.
rows.full: The accident model contains at least three coupled models: <Physical_Model> what trajectories and impacts are possible. <Perceptual_Model> what each participant could see or anticipate. <Normative_Model> which actions violated rules or produced responsibility.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The accident model contains at least three coupled models: <Physical_Model> what trajectories and impacts are possible.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A contemporary AI world model should not be evaluated only by whether its output resembles reality.
rows.full: A contemporary AI world model should not be evaluated only by whether its output resembles reality. It should declare: <Which_Behavior_Is_Being_Modeled> <Which_Variables_Correspond> <Which_Relations_Are_Invariant> <Which_Interventions_Are_Supported> <Which_Consequences_Transfer>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A contemporary AI world model should not be evaluated only by whether its output resembles reality.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The courtroom setup is proto-cinematic: objects are arranged, moved through phases, viewed from positions, compared against testimony, and used to construct counterfactual sequences.
rows.full: The courtroom setup is proto-cinematic: objects are arranged, moved through phases, viewed from positions, compared against testimony, and used to construct counterfactual sequences. A model becomes cinema when its possible states are organized as consequential time.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The courtroom setup is proto-cinematic: objects are arranged, moved through phases, viewed from positions, compared against testimony, and used to construct counterfactual sequences.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Physical similarity can model the collision, but thick description is needed to model the accident as accusation, negligence, testimony, injury, or injustice.
rows.full: Physical similarity can model the collision, but thick description is needed to model the accident as accusation, negligence, testimony, injury, or injustice. One event supports multiple models because different institutions ask different questions of it.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Physical similarity can model the collision, but thick description is needed to model the accident as accusation, negligence, testimony, injury, or injustice.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: There are symbols in science as surely as in poetry.
rows.full: There are symbols in science as surely as in poetry. The difference is not: <Poetry> = symbolic <Science> = literal. The stronger distinction is: <Poetry> organizes appearances and possible meanings. <Science> organizes testable relations through formalized symbols. <Religion> organizes conceptions and dispositions under a claim of ultimate reality.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: There are symbols in science as surely as in poetry.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Symbolic_Form> → <Conception> → <Interpretive_Practice> → <Disposition> → <Action> → <Experienced_Confirmation> → <Reinforced_Symbolic_Order>.
rows.full: <Symbolic_Form> → <Conception> → <Interpretive_Practice> → <Disposition> → <Action> → <Experienced_Confirmation> → <Reinforced_Symbolic_Order>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Symbolic_Form> → <Conception> → <Interpretive_Practice> → <Disposition> → <Action> → <Experienced_Confirmation> → <Reinforced_Symbolic_Order>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Model_Of> represents how some domain is understood.
rows.full: <Model_Of> represents how some domain is understood. <Model_For> directs how a domain should be produced, inhabited, or transformed. A religious cosmology is both. So is an architectural plan, legal code, scientific simulation, economic forecast, or AI classifier.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Model_Of> represents how some domain is understood.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Science generally asks: <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Would_Disconfirm_Them?> <What_Follows_Under_Specified_Conditions?> Religion generally asks…
rows.full: Science generally asks: <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Would_Disconfirm_Them?> <What_Follows_Under_Specified_Conditions?> Religion generally asks: <What_Is_Ultimately_Real?> <How_Should_One_Live_In_Light_Of_It?> <How_Can_Suffering_And_Disorder_Be_Endured_Or_Interpreted?> Both questions are symbolically mediated.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Science generally asks: <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Would_Disconfirm_Them?> <What_Follows_Under_Specified_Conditions?> Religion generally asks…

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A world model is never merely a model of the world once institutions act through it.
rows.full: A world model is never merely a model of the world once institutions act through it. It becomes a model for the world: <Prediction> → <Decision> → <Intervention> → <Changed_Reality> → <New_Training_Data>. Representation becomes governance.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A world model is never merely a model of the world once institutions act through it.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: An AI-generated risk score, emotion label, behavioral forecast, or synthetic environment acquires an “aura of factuality” when embedded in institutional rituals…
rows.full: An AI-generated risk score, emotion label, behavioral forecast, or synthetic environment acquires an “aura of factuality” when embedded in institutional rituals: <Data_Collection> → <Model_Output> → <Professional_Dashboard> → <Official_Decision> → <Material_Consequence>. The interface can make a contested interpretation appear as an observed fact.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: An AI-generated risk score, emotion label, behavioral forecast, or synthetic environment acquires an “aura of factuality” when embedded in institutional rituals…

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Wittgensteinian models require correlations that let a representation touch reality.
rows.full: Wittgensteinian models require correlations that let a representation touch reality. Geertz adds that public symbolic systems determine why those correlations matter, how they are interpreted, and which actions they authorize. <Model> + <Cultural_Use> + <Institutional_Authority> → <Social_Reality>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Wittgensteinian models require correlations that let a representation touch reality.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Film can operate as both model of and model for experience.
rows.full: Film can operate as both model of and model for experience. It shows a world while training viewers in how to recognize danger, intimacy, heroism, normality, causation, and emotional resolution.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Film can operate as both model of and model for experience.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The paper provides evidence that language models can become effective learned emulators of bounded symbolic environments when trained directly on action-conditioned transition histories.
rows.full: The paper provides evidence that language models can become effective learned emulators of bounded symbolic environments when trained directly on action-conditioned transition histories. That is a significant result, but it is narrower than showing that language models possess general internal models of the physical or social world.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The paper provides evidence that language models can become effective learned emulators of bounded symbolic environments when trained directly on action-conditioned transition histories.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Real_Interaction_Trajectories> → <Action_State_Dialogues> → <Dynamics_Aligned_Supervised_Training> → <History_Conditioned_Transition_Model> → <Predicted_Observation_And_Reward> → <Agent_Action> → <Extended_Synthetic_Trajectory>.
rows.full: <Real_Interaction_Trajectories> → <Action_State_Dialogues> → <Dynamics_Aligned_Supervised_Training> → <History_Conditioned_Transition_Model> → <Predicted_Observation_And_Reward> → <Agent_Action> → <Extended_Synthetic_Trajectory>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Real_Interaction_Trajectories> → <Action_State_Dialogues> → <Dynamics_Aligned_Supervised_Training> → <History_Conditioned_Transition_Model> → <Predicted_Observation_And_Reward> → <Agent_Action> → <Extended_Synthetic_Trajectory>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Text_Continuation_Model> predicts likely language.
rows.full: <Text_Continuation_Model> predicts likely language. <Protocol_Emulator> reproduces responses under a fixed interaction format. <State_Tracking_Emulator> maintains relevant variables across turns. <Counterfactual_Model> correctly distinguishes outcomes under alternative actions. <Interactive_Simulator> sustains closed-loop trajectories across diverse policies. <General_World_Model> transfers its causal structure across environments, interfaces, and embodiments. The paper strongly supports the middle categories in structured text domains.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Text_Continuation_Model> predicts likely language.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Level_1> One-step textual fidelity.
rows.full: <Level_1> One-step textual fidelity. <Level_2> Long-horizon state consistency. <Level_3> Robustness under unfamiliar agents and environments. <Level_4> Correctly different consequences for controlled alternative actions. <Level_5> Resistance to simulator exploitation. <Level_6> Transfer of imagined policies into independently grounded environments. <Level_7> Measurable downstream agent utility.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Level_1> One-step textual fidelity.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The strongest evidence of world modeling would take the form: <Same_History> + <Action_A> → <Correct_State_A> and <Same_History> + <Action_B> → <Correctly_Different_State_B>.
rows.full: The strongest evidence of world modeling would take the form: <Same_History> + <Action_A> → <Correct_State_A> and <Same_History> + <Action_B> → <Correctly_Different_State_B>. Prediction of ordinary next states is weaker evidence than accurate intervention-sensitive divergence.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The strongest evidence of world modeling would take the form: <Same_History> + <Action_A> → <Correct_State_A> and <Same_History> + <Action_B> → <Correctly_Different_State_B>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The textual environment is a cultural program: it defines valid entities, permissible actions, success, failure, and which state changes count as meaningful.
rows.full: The textual environment is a cultural program: it defines valid entities, permissible actions, success, failure, and which state changes count as meaningful. The model learns both a model of this world and a model for acting within it.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The textual environment is a cultural program: it defines valid entities, permissible actions, success, failure, and which state changes count as meaningful.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The textual state does not resemble the complete underlying environment.
rows.full: The textual state does not resemble the complete underlying environment. It represents it because the protocol establishes operational correspondences between: <Textual_Signs> <Environment_States> <Permitted_Actions> <Resulting_Transitions>. The interface is a calibrated model, not a transparent copy.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The textual state does not resemble the complete underlying environment.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Once the simulator verifies actions and manufactures training experience, it governs which futures the agent considers plausible, which risks it avoids, and which behaviors it rehearses.
rows.full: Once the simulator verifies actions and manufactures training experience, it governs which futures the agent considers plausible, which risks it avoids, and which behaviors it rehearses. A world model becomes a constitutional layer for the policy.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Once the simulator verifies actions and manufactures training experience, it governs which futures the agent considers plausible, which risks it avoids, and which behaviors it rehearses.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: An idea is not fully understood by defining what it represents.
rows.full: An idea is not fully understood by defining what it represents. It must be translated into a conditional program: <Given_Conditions> + <Proposed_Operation> → <Expected_Consequences>. Its meaning becomes clearer through this projected difference, and its validity becomes clearer when the difference is tested.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: An idea is not fully understood by defining what it represents.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Indeterminate_Situation> → <Selective_Description> → <Hypothesis> → <Projected_Consequences> → <Experimental_Action> → <Observed_Consequences> → <Situation_Reconstructed_Or_Hypothesis_Revised>.
rows.full: <Indeterminate_Situation> → <Selective_Description> → <Hypothesis> → <Projected_Consequences> → <Experimental_Action> → <Observed_Consequences> → <Situation_Reconstructed_Or_Hypothesis_Revised>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Indeterminate_Situation> → <Selective_Description> → <Hypothesis> → <Projected_Consequences> → <Experimental_Action> → <Observed_Consequences> → <Situation_Reconstructed_Or_Hypothesis_Revised>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Peirce> asks what general habits of conduct a concept implies.
rows.full: <Peirce> asks what general habits of conduct a concept implies. <James> asks what concrete experiential difference adopting a belief makes. <Dewey> asks how concepts function as instruments for transforming problematic situations.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Peirce> asks what general habits of conduct a concept implies.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Meaning> concerns the conceivable consequences of using a concept.
rows.full: <Meaning> concerns the conceivable consequences of using a concept. <Truth> concerns whether the projected consequences survive inquiry and experience. <Value> concerns whether the resulting transformation creates a more desirable situation. These should not be collapsed into one criterion.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Meaning> concerns the conceivable consequences of using a concept.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A world model is pragmatically meaningful only if it supports counterfactual action: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence>.
rows.full: A world model is pragmatically meaningful only if it supports counterfactual action: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence>. Its value is not exhausted by producing plausible descriptions. It must help an agent anticipate, test, and revise its conduct.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A world model is pragmatically meaningful only if it supports counterfactual action: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Sterrett’s model touches reality through correlations and preserved transformations.
rows.full: Sterrett’s model touches reality through correlations and preserved transformations. Pragmatism adds the experimental criterion: A model’s meaning lies in the consequences its use licenses, and its truth is tested by whether those consequences survive contact with the modeled situation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Sterrett’s model touches reality through correlations and preserved transformations.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Geertz’s symbols are models of and models for reality.
rows.full: Geertz’s symbols are models of and models for reality. Dewey explains the transition between them: <Symbolic_Conception> → <Guided_Action> → <Changed_Experience> → <Revised_Conception>. Cultural symbols do not merely represent worlds; they participate in making them.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Geertz’s symbols are models of and models for reality.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A cinematic image is not only a representation to be interpreted.
rows.full: A cinematic image is not only a representation to be interpreted. It can be an instrument that reorganizes perception, expectation, emotion, and future action. The pragmatic question is: <What_Difference_Does_This_Image_Make_In_Subsequent_Experience?>
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A cinematic image is not only a representation to be interpreted.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Youngblood’s feedback loop becomes pragmatist when machine-generated variation is evaluated through its consequences for subsequent creation: <Proposal> → <Perceptual_Experience> → <Judgment> → <Revised_Operation>.
rows.full: Youngblood’s feedback loop becomes pragmatist when machine-generated variation is evaluated through its consequences for subsequent creation: <Proposal> → <Perceptual_Experience> → <Judgment> → <Revised_Operation>. The system becomes intelligent not by generating endlessly, but by supporting consequential revision.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Youngblood’s feedback loop becomes pragmatist when machine-generated variation is evaluated through its consequences for subsequent creation: <Proposal> → <Perceptual_Experience> → <Judgment> → <Revised_Operation>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Cinema is not primarily a machine for recording objects.
rows.full: Cinema is not primarily a machine for recording objects. It is a machine for differentiating functions within a field of change: <Perceive> <Be_Affected> <Act> <Relate> <Remember> <Expose_Time>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Cinema is not primarily a machine for recording objects.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Set_Or_Frame> selects a relatively closed collection of elements.
rows.full: <Set_Or_Frame> selects a relatively closed collection of elements. <Shot_Or_Movement> transforms the relations among those elements. <Whole_Or_Open> changes qualitatively as movements connect with what exceeds the frame. The shot is therefore not merely content inside a rectangle. It mediates between a selected system and an open whole undergoing change.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Set_Or_Frame> selects a relatively closed collection of elements.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Acentred_Field_Of_Movements> → <Interval> → <Selective_Perception> → <Intensive_Affection> → <Possible_Action> → <Changed_Milieu> → <New_Perception>.
rows.full: <Acentred_Field_Of_Movements> → <Interval> → <Selective_Perception> → <Intensive_Affection> → <Possible_Action> → <Changed_Milieu> → <New_Perception>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Acentred_Field_Of_Movements> → <Interval> → <Selective_Perception> → <Intensive_Affection> → <Possible_Action> → <Changed_Milieu> → <New_Perception>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Perception_Image> selects what matters for possible action.
rows.full: <Perception_Image> selects what matters for possible action. <Affection_Image> holds intensive potential between stimulus and response. <Impulse_Image> expresses drives operating between originary worlds and derived milieus. <Action_Image> actualizes forces through situated behaviour. <Mental_Image> makes relations, interpretations, laws, and signs themselves into the object of cinema.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Perception_Image> selects what matters for possible action.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Whitney asks: <How_Shall_Motion_Pattern_Time?> Deleuze asks: <What_Function_Does_Movement_Perform_As_An_Image?> Whitney supplies a compositional theory of differential rate, resonance, tension, and resolution.
rows.full: Whitney asks: <How_Shall_Motion_Pattern_Time?> Deleuze asks: <What_Function_Does_Movement_Perform_As_An_Image?> Whitney supplies a compositional theory of differential rate, resonance, tension, and resolution. Deleuze supplies an ontological-semiotic theory of perception, affection, action, relation, and time. Together they yield: <Temporal_Pattern> + <Image_Function> = <Cinematic_Thought>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Whitney asks: <How_Shall_Motion_Pattern_Time?> Deleuze asks: <What_Function_Does_Movement_Perform_As_An_Image?> Whitney supplies a compositional theory of differential rate, resonance, tension, and resolution.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Knowlton provides executable visual operations.
rows.full: Knowlton provides executable visual operations. Deleuze provides the image-regimes those operations can instantiate. A line shift might function as: <Perception> when it reveals a field. <Affection> when it becomes pure intensity. <Action> when it modifies a situation. <Relation> when it makes a rule or connection visible. The command does not determine the cinematic sign; its role within the sensory-motor structure does.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Knowlton provides executable visual operations.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Deleuze explains how a body selects, feels, and acts within a milieu.
rows.full: Deleuze explains how a body selects, feels, and acts within a milieu. Geertz asks what those actions mean within public symbolic systems. A complete cultural cinema model therefore requires: <Physical_Movement> → <Perceptual_Selection> → <Affective_Intensity> → <Situated_Action> → <Public_Interpretation> → <Institutional_Consequence>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Deleuze explains how a body selects, feels, and acts within a milieu.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The classical action-image resembles pragmatic inquiry: <Problematic_Situation> → <Perception> → <Hypothesis_Or_Response> → <Action> → <Transformed_Situation>.
rows.full: The classical action-image resembles pragmatic inquiry: <Problematic_Situation> → <Perception> → <Hypothesis_Or_Response> → <Action> → <Transformed_Situation>. Deleuze contributes the failure case pragmatism often backgrounds: situations in which perception is clichéd, action is impossible, consequences are disconnected, and thought begins only because the sensory-motor circuit breaks.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The classical action-image resembles pragmatic inquiry: <Problematic_Situation> → <Perception> → <Hypothesis_Or_Response> → <Action> → <Transformed_Situation>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Most contemporary world models are implicitly action-image machines.
rows.full: Most contemporary world models are implicitly action-image machines. They seek to predict: <Observation> + <Action> → <Next_State>. Deleuze shows that this is only one regime of images. A cinematic world model must also represent: <Selective_Perception> <Unresolved_Affect> <Impulse> <Ambiguous_Relation> <Failure_Of_Action> <Direct_Duration>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Most contemporary world models are implicitly action-image machines.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A branch should not be defined only as a different event path.
rows.full: A branch should not be defined only as a different event path. It can alter the image-regime itself: <Perception_Branch> changes what can be noticed. <Affection_Branch> changes which potentials intensify. <Action_Branch> changes what is done. <Relation_Branch> changes how events are interpreted. <Time_Branch> suspends action and exposes duration directly.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A branch should not be defined only as a different event path.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: To direct is not merely to choose content or camera position.
rows.full: To direct is not merely to choose content or camera position. It is to determine what kind of image a movement becomes and how one regime passes into another. [direct] = [shape] the passage among {<Perception>, <Affection>, <Impulse>, <Action>, <Relation>, <Time>}.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: To direct is not merely to choose content or camera position.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The file is assembling a textual control theory for generative media: <Thick_Prompt> establishes the meaningful conditions of a world.
rows.full: The file is assembling a textual control theory for generative media: <Thick_Prompt> establishes the meaningful conditions of a world. <Generative_Model> renders or evolves that world. <Operative_Description> externalizes the model’s current state into an editable specification. <Revision> changes the world through textual intervention.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The file is assembling a textual control theory for generative media: <Thick_Prompt> establishes the meaningful conditions of a world.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Thick prompting is the specification of the contextual relations that make generated events meaningful and consequential.
rows.full: Thick prompting is the specification of the contextual relations that make generated events meaningful and consequential. It supplies not simply: <Objects> <Appearance> <Motion>, but: <Intentions> <Social_Codes> <Roles> <Histories> <Institutional_Conditions> <Stakes> <Expected_Consequences> <Competing_Interpretations>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Thick prompting is the specification of the contextual relations that make generated events meaningful and consequential.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Operative description is a machine-actionable account of a generated artifact or world state whose terms can be edited to produce controlled transformations in that artifact or its subsequent evolution.
rows.full: Operative description is a machine-actionable account of a generated artifact or world state whose terms can be edited to produce controlled transformations in that artifact or its subsequent evolution. Its value depends upon whether the description preserves: <Entity_Identity> <State> <Relations> <Constraints> <Causal_Dependencies> <Permitted_Operations> <Transformation_History>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Operative description is a machine-actionable account of a generated artifact or world state whose terms can be edited to produce controlled transformations in that artifact or its subsequent evolution.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Caption> says what an artifact appears to contain.
rows.full: <Caption> says what an artifact appears to contain. <Prompt> requests an artifact. <Specification> states the conditions the artifact must satisfy. <Operative_Description> exposes those conditions after or during generation so they can be revised and reapplied.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Caption> says what an artifact appears to contain.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The generative imagetext should not be identified with a hidden embedding alone.
rows.full: The generative imagetext should not be identified with a hidden embedding alone. It is better understood as the complete operational relation among: <Textual_Instruction> <Visual_Output> <Model_State> <Editing_History> <Interpretive_Context> <Available_Transformations>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The generative imagetext should not be identified with a hidden embedding alone.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: Once the generated artifact is treated as a world, thick prompting must specify more than appearance: <What_Exists> <What_Persists> <What_Entities_Know> <What_Actions_Mean> <What_Actions_Change> <What_Rules_Cannot_Be_Violated> <What_Remains_Contested>.
rows.full: Once the generated artifact is treated as a world, thick prompting must specify more than appearance: <What_Exists> <What_Persists> <What_Entities_Know> <What_Actions_Mean> <What_Actions_Change> <What_Rules_Cannot_Be_Violated> <What_Remains_Contested>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Once the generated artifact is treated as a world, thick prompting must specify more than appearance: <What_Exists> <What_Persists> <What_Entities_Know> <What_Actions_Mean> <What_Actions_Change> <What_Rules_Cannot_Be_Violated> <What_Remains_Contested>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A thick prompt should not force one interpretation to become unquestionable fact.
rows.full: A thick prompt should not force one interpretation to become unquestionable fact. It should be capable of preserving: <Actor_Interpretation> <Observer_Interpretation> <Institutional_Interpretation> <Counter_Interpretation>. Otherwise contextual richness becomes semantic authoritarianism.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A thick prompt should not force one interpretation to become unquestionable fact.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
