rows.kernel: Operative ekphrasis gives language productive force: text causes an image to appear.
rows.full: Operative ekphrasis gives language productive force: text causes an image to appear. Operative description adds a persistent control requirement: the resulting image or world must return an editable textual account capable of directing its next transformation. `Operative ekphrasis the collapse of the text image distinction in multimodal AI.pdf`
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Operative ekphrasis gives language productive force: text causes an image to appear.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The project requires a layered ontology rather than one analogy: <Surface> what is visibly or verbally rendered.
rows.full: The project requires a layered ontology rather than one analogy: <Surface> what is visibly or verbally rendered. <State> what currently exists in the represented domain. <Dynamics> how state may change. <Action> what interventions are available. <Constraint> what must remain invariant. <Simulation> the execution of state transitions. <World_Model> the learned mechanism that predicts those transitions. <World_Text> the symbolic specification through which those entities, relations, actions, and transition constraints become expressible and editable.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The project requires a layered ontology rather than one analogy: <Surface> what is visibly or verbally rendered.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <Image> a perceptible visual configuration.
rows.full: <Image> a perceptible visual configuration. <Image_Model> a generative distribution over visual configurations. <Video> a visible temporal sequence. <Video_Model> a generative distribution over temporal visual sequences. <World> a domain of persistent entities, relations, affordances, rules, and consequences. <World_Model> a predictive model of how that domain changes under endogenous and exogenous interventions. <World_Text> a symbolic, interpretable, and executable specification of that domain and its possible transformations.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Image> a perceptible visual configuration.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A <Video_Model> answers: <What_Visible_Sequence_Is_Likely_To_Follow?> A <World_Model> answers: <What_State_Follows_If_This_Action_Is_Taken?> A <World_Text> must answer…
rows.full: A <Video_Model> answers: <What_Visible_Sequence_Is_Likely_To_Follow?> A <World_Model> answers: <What_State_Follows_If_This_Action_Is_Taken?> A <World_Text> must answer: <What_Exists?> <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Actions_Are_Possible?> <What_Changes?> <What_Must_Persist?> <What_Remains_Underdetermined?>
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A <Video_Model> answers: <What_Visible_Sequence_Is_Likely_To_Follow?> A <World_Model> answers: <What_State_Follows_If_This_Action_Is_Taken?> A <World_Text> must answer…

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A future definition of <World_Text> should preserve the lesson of <Imagetext>: the symbolic and simulated layers are not independent channels later connected.
rows.full: A future definition of <World_Text> should preserve the lesson of <Imagetext>: the symbolic and simulated layers are not independent channels later connected. They co-produce what the world can be perceived as, acted upon as, and interpreted as.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A future definition of <World_Text> should preserve the lesson of <Imagetext>: the symbolic and simulated layers are not independent channels later connected.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A future definition of <World_Text> must also preserve the operational structure of a world model: <Current_State> + <Action> → <Predicted_Next_State>.
rows.full: A future definition of <World_Text> must also preserve the operational structure of a world model: <Current_State> + <Action> → <Predicted_Next_State>. Without transition logic, it remains descriptive text rather than world-text.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A future definition of <World_Text> must also preserve the operational structure of a world model: <Current_State> + <Action> → <Predicted_Next_State>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A world-text cannot contain only objects and physics.
rows.full: A world-text cannot contain only objects and physics. It must also encode or expose: <Actor_Roles> <Intentions> <Social_Codes> <Institutional_Rules> <Competing_Interpretations> <Consequences_Of_Classification>. Otherwise it models motion without modeling meaning.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A world-text cannot contain only objects and physics.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The meaning of a world-text lies in what transformations it enables and what consequences follow from using it.
rows.full: The meaning of a world-text lies in what transformations it enables and what consequences follow from using it. A valid world-text must therefore be testable through intervention, not merely readable as description.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The meaning of a world-text lies in what transformations it enables and what consequences follow from using it.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A world-text should not reduce cinema to action-conditioned state prediction.
rows.full: A world-text should not reduce cinema to action-conditioned state prediction. It must also be capable of specifying: <Perception> <Affection> <Impulse> <Action> <Relation> <Crisis_Of_Action> <Duration>. A world is not only what changes under action; it is also what becomes perceptible when action fails.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A world-text should not reduce cinema to action-conditioned state prediction.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The project should not define <Worldtext> as text that describes a world or even text that generates one.
rows.full: The project should not define <Worldtext> as text that describes a world or even text that generates one. It should define the term around persistent operational binding: <Symbolic_Expression> ↔ <World_State> ↔ <Permitted_Transition> ↔ <Rendered_Experience>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The project should not define <Worldtext> as text that describes a world or even text that generates one.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: <World_Description> human-readable account of entities, situations, meanings, and stakes.
rows.full: <World_Description> human-readable account of entities, situations, meanings, and stakes. <World_Specification> machine-interpretable state, relations, constraints, affordances, and transition conditions. <World_Model> learned or explicit mechanism predicting state change. <Simulator> process that executes transitions through time. <Renderer> process that converts state into image, video, sound, or text. <Worldtext> the editable symbolic layer that binds description, specification, and evolving simulated state.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <World_Description> human-readable account of entities, situations, meanings, and stakes.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The useful relation is not: <Image> : <Image_Model> as <Video> : <World_Model>.
rows.full: The useful relation is not: <Image> : <Image_Model> as <Video> : <World_Model>. It is: <Imagetext> binds visual and verbal representation. <Worldtext> binds symbolic articulation and executable world dynamics. <World_Model> predicts or generates the dynamics to which the worldtext is bound.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The useful relation is not: <Image> : <Image_Model> as <Video> : <World_Model>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A credible world model should demonstrate: <State_Persistence> <Action_Sensitivity> <Counterfactual_Difference> <Constraint_Consistency> <Long_Horizon_Consequences> <Uncertainty_Representation> <Transfer_Beyond_Training_Trajectories>.
rows.full: A credible world model should demonstrate: <State_Persistence> <Action_Sensitivity> <Counterfactual_Difference> <Constraint_Consistency> <Long_Horizon_Consequences> <Uncertainty_Representation> <Transfer_Beyond_Training_Trajectories>.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A credible world model should demonstrate: <State_Persistence> <Action_Sensitivity> <Counterfactual_Difference> <Constraint_Consistency> <Long_Horizon_Consequences> <Uncertainty_Representation> <Transfer_Beyond_Training_Trajectories>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A credible worldtext should allow a user or agent to: [inspect] the current world state.
rows.full: A credible worldtext should allow a user or agent to: [inspect] the current world state. [describe] entities and relations. [edit] a local condition. [preserve] declared invariants. [simulate] the resulting consequences. [trace] why the world changed. [contest] interpretations that are not objective state.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A credible worldtext should allow a user or agent to: [inspect] the current world state.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The relation between language and world must remain heterogeneous.
rows.full: The relation between language and world must remain heterogeneous. Text should not be treated as a perfectly transparent encoding of state, just as the visual and verbal do not collapse into one pure representational substance.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The relation between language and world must remain heterogeneous.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The system must preserve the action-conditioned predictive structure: <Latent_Or_Explicit_State> + <Action> → <Distribution_Over_Next_States>.
rows.full: The system must preserve the action-conditioned predictive structure: <Latent_Or_Explicit_State> + <Action> → <Distribution_Over_Next_States>. Without this, worldtext becomes scene description rather than an interface to a world model.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The system must preserve the action-conditioned predictive structure: <Latent_Or_Explicit_State> + <Action> → <Distribution_Over_Next_States>.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: A human world requires more than entities and mechanics.
rows.full: A human world requires more than entities and mechanics. The worldtext must represent how the same observable event may carry different meanings for different participants, communities, and institutions.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: A human world requires more than entities and mechanics.

rows.label: SO_WHAT
rows.kernel: The worldtext should not reduce all cinematic change to successful action.
rows.full: The worldtext should not reduce all cinematic change to successful action. It must also represent perception, affect, delay, failed action, ambiguous relation, and durations in which no useful intervention occurs.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The worldtext should not reduce all cinematic change to successful action.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Does repeated prompting make people better at noticing and describing the world, or merely better at predicting what a particular model will generate?
rows.full: Does repeated prompting make people better at noticing and describing the world, or merely better at predicting what a particular model will generate?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Does repeated prompting make people better at noticing and describing the world, or merely better at predicting what a particular model will generate?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The argument assumes that greater prompt specificity produces a closer translation of the user’s internal image.
rows.full: The argument assumes that greater prompt specificity produces a closer translation of the user’s internal image. In practice, specificity may instead activate stronger dataset stereotypes, giving the appearance of control while moving the result toward conventional imagery.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The argument assumes that greater prompt specificity produces a closer translation of the user’s internal image.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Compare how the same users describe one imagined scene for another person and for several image models.
rows.full: Compare how the same users describe one imagined scene for another person and for several image models. Track which words survive, which disappear, how outputs differ across languages and whether model-trained prompting habits transfer to non-instrumental writing. The decisive evidence would be whether prompting expands perceptual vocabulary or standardizes it around machine-legible aesthetics.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Compare how the same users describe one imagined scene for another person and for several image models.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: How much prompting labor, domain knowledge, iteration and output selection are required before the model stops producing bland material—and who possesses the time and expertise to provide that labor?
rows.full: How much prompting labor, domain knowledge, iteration and output selection are required before the model stops producing bland material—and who possesses the time and expertise to provide that labor?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: How much prompting labor, domain knowledge, iteration and output selection are required before the model stops producing bland material—and who possesses the time and expertise to provide that labor?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that the existence of a prompt capable of eliciting a distinctive result demonstrates that the model itself is not homogenizing.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that the existence of a prompt capable of eliciting a distinctive result demonstrates that the model itself is not homogenizing. This confuses recoverable variation somewhere in the model’s output space with the probability that ordinary users will reach it.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that the existence of a prompt capable of eliciting a distinctive result demonstrates that the model itself is not homogenizing.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Run preregistered comparisons using identical tasks across default prompts, prompts written by ordinary users, prompts written by professional creators and prompts optimized by specialists.
rows.full: Run preregistered comparisons using identical tasks across default prompts, prompts written by ordinary users, prompts written by professional creators and prompts optimized by specialists. Blindly assess originality, quality and similarity to training conventions while recording iteration count, elapsed time, token cost and human editing. The conclusion should change materially depending on whether expert prompting produces consistent gains or merely enables selective presentation of rare successes.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Run preregistered comparisons using identical tasks across default prompts, prompts written by ordinary users, prompts written by professional creators and prompts optimized by specialists.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Can a solo AI-assisted transmedia universe remain artistically coherent, economically sustainable, and personally manageable after the novelty of rapid creation gives way to years of maintenance?
rows.full: Can a solo AI-assisted transmedia universe remain artistically coherent, economically sustainable, and personally manageable after the novelty of rapid creation gives way to years of maintenance?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Can a solo AI-assisted transmedia universe remain artistically coherent, economically sustainable, and personally manageable after the novelty of rapid creation gives way to years of maintenance?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that production was the principal barrier preventing the project from existing.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that production was the principal barrier preventing the project from existing. The larger barrier may eventually be audience formation, trust, distribution, rights management, recurring maintenance, and the creator’s finite capacity to judge an effectively unlimited stream of possible content.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that production was the principal barrier preventing the project from existing.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Investigate the project as a longitudinal production system rather than as a launch story.
rows.full: Investigate the project as a longitudinal production system rather than as a launch story. Measure output volume, discarded generations, human labor hours, tool costs, audience retention, canon errors, platform dependencies, contributor displacement, and the proportion of published material substantially rewritten by the creator. The decisive test is not whether AI enables one person to begin a universe, but whether it enables that person to govern one without becoming its exhausted administrative bottleneck.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Investigate the project as a longitudinal production system rather than as a launch story.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: What cinematic form—not merely what screenplay choices—could reproduce the poem’s unstable movement among narration, memory, fabrication, divine causation, and direct experience?
rows.full: What cinematic form—not merely what screenplay choices—could reproduce the poem’s unstable movement among narration, memory, fabrication, divine causation, and direct experience?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What cinematic form—not merely what screenplay choices—could reproduce the poem’s unstable movement among narration, memory, fabrication, divine causation, and direct experience?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The article protects the assumption that the feature-length prestige blockbuster is the relevant container for the Odyssey.
rows.full: The article protects the assumption that the feature-length prestige blockbuster is the relevant container for the Odyssey. Yet the poem’s episodic architecture, shifting narrators, recursive storytelling, oral repetition, and competing versions may be fundamentally better suited to serial, interactive, performative, or multiple-perspective forms.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The article protects the assumption that the feature-length prestige blockbuster is the relevant container for the Odyssey.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Investigate adaptation structure rather than adaptation fidelity.
rows.full: Investigate adaptation structure rather than adaptation fidelity. Compare linear feature films with serialized, polyphonic, interactive, and explicitly oral-performance models. Test whether audiences respond differently when Odysseus’ stories are presented as unverifiable self-narration, when divine events retain competing explanations, and when the slaughter at Ithaca is shown from the perspectives of Penelope, Telemachus, the suitors, and the enslaved women. The decisive question is not how accurately cinema can illustrate Homer, but what medium arrangement can preserve the poem’s conflict among incompatible truths.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Investigate adaptation structure rather than adaptation fidelity.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: What enforceable institutional test distinguishes a genuine centaur arrangement from a reverse-centaur system marketed as “human in the loop”?
rows.full: What enforceable institutional test distinguishes a genuine centaur arrangement from a reverse-centaur system marketed as “human in the loop”?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What enforceable institutional test distinguishes a genuine centaur arrangement from a reverse-centaur system marketed as “human in the loop”?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that workers can meaningfully resist once they understand the economic structure.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that workers can meaningfully resist once they understand the economic structure. That assumes unions, regulators, and public institutions retain sufficient bargaining power, technical access, and jurisdiction over firms able to move capital, data, and operations across borders.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that workers can meaningfully resist once they understand the economic structure.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Investigate AI deployments at the workflow level across hospitals, warehouses, publishing, education, customer service, and software development.
rows.full: Investigate AI deployments at the workflow level across hospitals, warehouses, publishing, education, customer service, and software development. Compare management claims with changes in headcount, wages, task discretion, error rates, surveillance, cognitive load, and liability. The decisive evidence is not whether an AI tool improves a benchmark, but whether the people working beside it gain greater authority over their work or become increasingly responsible for failures they no longer have the power to prevent.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Investigate AI deployments at the workflow level across hospitals, warehouses, publishing, education, customer service, and software development.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: What measurable capability did Fable 5 provide beyond existing open models, conventional security tools, public vulnerability databases, and competent human researchers—and did that marginal capability benefit attackers more than defenders?
rows.full: What measurable capability did Fable 5 provide beyond existing open models, conventional security tools, public vulnerability databases, and competent human researchers—and did that marginal capability benefit attackers more than defenders?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What measurable capability did Fable 5 provide beyond existing open models, conventional security tools, public vulnerability databases, and competent human researchers—and did that marginal capability benefit attackers more than defenders?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The government appears to protect the assumption that restricting access to one frontier model meaningfully restricts the underlying capability.
rows.full: The government appears to protect the assumption that restricting access to one frontier model meaningfully restricts the underlying capability. The counterargument protects the opposite assumption: that defensive framing makes the capability safe enough to distribute. Neither assumption is established by the reported prompt exchange.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The government appears to protect the assumption that restricting access to one frontier model meaningfully restricts the underlying capability.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Release a redacted version of the research and reproduce the evaluation across closed, open-weight, domestic, and foreign models.
rows.full: Release a redacted version of the research and reproduce the evaluation across closed, open-weight, domestic, and foreign models. Measure vulnerability discovery, exploit generation, patch correctness, test quality, time saved, false positives, required human expertise, and performance under realistic attacker and defender workflows. The decisive comparison is the net change in security capacity—not whether a model answered “fix this code.”
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Release a redacted version of the research and reproduce the evaluation across closed, open-weight, domestic, and foreign models.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: When a neural CAD model infers unstated geometry, constraints, materials, or performance requirements and the resulting design fails, who is professionally, contractually, and legally responsible for the inference?
rows.full: When a neural CAD model infers unstated geometry, constraints, materials, or performance requirements and the resulting design fails, who is professionally, contractually, and legally responsible for the inference?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: When a neural CAD model infers unstated geometry, constraints, materials, or performance requirements and the resulting design fails, who is professionally, contractually, and legally responsible for the inference?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that design intent exists prior to representation and can be faithfully extracted from speech, sketches, images, and organizational data.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that design intent exists prior to representation and can be faithfully extracted from speech, sketches, images, and organizational data. In practice, designers often discover intent through the resistance of the tool, iterative modelling, failed constraints, and negotiation with collaborators. If friction is partly constitutive of reasoning, eliminating it may accelerate premature commitment rather than improve design.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that design intent exists prior to representation and can be faithfully extracted from speech, sketches, images, and organizational data.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Evaluate neural CAD on full revision lifecycles rather than isolated generation tasks.
rows.full: Evaluate neural CAD on full revision lifecycles rather than isolated generation tasks. Give professionals ambiguous briefs, require repeated requirement changes, and measure constraint integrity, manufacturability, code compliance, expert correction time, hidden assumptions, downstream interoperability, and recovery after erroneous generations. Compare novice, CAD-expert, and domain-expert users. The decisive test is not whether the system can create an impressive initial model, but whether humans can understand, challenge, revise, certify, and safely inherit what it creates.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Evaluate neural CAD on full revision lifecycles rather than isolated generation tasks.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: What socially valuable work will justify data centers consuming a projected fifth of U.S.
rows.full: What socially valuable work will justify data centers consuming a projected fifth of U.S. electricity, and who has authority to decide that this use outranks competing household, manufacturing, transportation, and decarbonization needs?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What socially valuable work will justify data centers consuming a projected fifth of U.S.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that projected AI demand should be satisfied rather than interrogated.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that projected AI demand should be satisfied rather than interrogated. If much of that demand reflects speculative overbuilding, duplicated model training, low-value inference, subsidized pricing, or investor signaling, then expanding the grid around it could socialize costs for infrastructure that later proves economically unnecessary.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that projected AI demand should be satisfied rather than interrogated.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Separate actual AI workloads from promotional forecasts.
rows.full: Separate actual AI workloads from promotional forecasts. Measure facility utilization, energy consumed per workload, revenue supported per megawatt, public subsidies, local price effects, emissions, water use, grid-upgrade responsibility, and the proportion of capacity reserved for speculative future demand. Compare those results with less energy-intensive architectures and non-AI uses of the same infrastructure. The decisive issue is not what constitutes AI’s backbone, but which parts of that backbone create enough verified value to deserve public and material support.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Separate actual AI workloads from promotional forecasts.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: At what point must the system return control to the user before a model-selected action is executed, and how should that consent be presented without training users to approve everything reflexively?
rows.full: At what point must the system return control to the user before a model-selected action is executed, and how should that consent be presented without training users to approve everything reflexively?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: At what point must the system return control to the user before a model-selected action is executed, and how should that consent be presented without training users to approve everything reflexively?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that the user’s initial request supplies sufficient consent for every downstream tool call the model infers.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that the user’s initial request supplies sufficient consent for every downstream tool call the model infers. That may hold for retrieving weather, but it breaks when an agent discovers new steps, changes scope, accesses sensitive information, communicates with third parties, or performs irreversible actions.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that the user’s initial request supplies sufficient consent for every downstream tool call the model infers.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Evaluate tool-calling systems through adversarial and longitudinal workflows rather than clean demonstrations.
rows.full: Evaluate tool-calling systems through adversarial and longitudinal workflows rather than clean demonstrations. Test ambiguous requests, malformed arguments, prompt injection inside tool results, duplicate execution, partial failure, stale state, conflicting tools, excessive permissions, and user interruption. Measure not only task completion but unauthorized actions, recoverability, consent comprehension, provenance, and whether users can distinguish a proposed action from one already executed.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Evaluate tool-calling systems through adversarial and longitudinal workflows rather than clean demonstrations.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Does GLM-5.2’s benchmark advantage survive when models are compared at equal task budgets for tokens, latency, hardware, energy, and permitted retries?
rows.full: Does GLM-5.2’s benchmark advantage survive when models are compared at equal task budgets for tokens, latency, hardware, energy, and permitted retries?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Does GLM-5.2’s benchmark advantage survive when models are compared at equal task budgets for tokens, latency, hardware, energy, and permitted retries?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that a higher aggregate score represents a generally stronger successor.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that a higher aggregate score represents a generally stronger successor. The opossum regression suggests that training changes may trade away specific representational, stylistic, or instruction-following abilities to improve benchmark-weighted behavior elsewhere.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that a higher aggregate score represents a generally stronger successor.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Run paired evaluations of GLM-5.1 and GLM-5.2 across a broad preserved task suite, including SVG composition, animation integrity, long-context retrieval, repository-scale coding, multilingual reasoning, instruction fidelity, and repeated-generation variance.
rows.full: Run paired evaluations of GLM-5.1 and GLM-5.2 across a broad preserved task suite, including SVG composition, animation integrity, long-context retrieval, repository-scale coding, multilingual reasoning, instruction fidelity, and repeated-generation variance. Report task success alongside output tokens, latency, hosting configuration, memory requirements, and human correction time. The decisive question is not whether GLM-5.2 leads an index, but which capabilities improved, which regressed, and what resources were exchanged for those gains.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Run paired evaluations of GLM-5.1 and GLM-5.2 across a broad preserved task suite, including SVG composition, animation integrity, long-context retrieval, repository-scale coding, multilingual reasoning, instruction fidelity, and repeated-generation variance.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: What observation would cause Altman to conclude that additional scaling is no longer the dominant path to more capable systems?
rows.full: What observation would cause Altman to conclude that additional scaling is no longer the dominant path to more capable systems?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What observation would cause Altman to conclude that additional scaling is no longer the dominant path to more capable systems?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that recent capability gains reveal a continuing underlying law rather than a temporary regime produced by increasing compute, improved post-training, better tools…
rows.full: The protected assumption is that recent capability gains reveal a continuing underlying law rather than a temporary regime produced by increasing compute, improved post-training, better tools, richer evaluation scaffolds, and selective presentation of successes.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that recent capability gains reveal a continuing underlying law rather than a temporary regime produced by increasing compute, improved post-training, better tools…

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Evaluate successive model generations under controlled compute, data, tool-access, and inference-time budgets.
rows.full: Evaluate successive model generations under controlled compute, data, tool-access, and inference-time budgets. Distinguish gains attributable to parameter scaling from those produced by reinforcement learning, retrieval, search, external verification, and agent scaffolding. Measure not only solved tasks but research-horizon length, error recovery, judgment under ambiguity, reproducibility, and cost per verified discovery. The decisive evidence is not whether scaling has worked, but whether its marginal returns remain superior to competing research directions.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Evaluate successive model generations under controlled compute, data, tool-access, and inference-time budgets.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Who specifically would have captured the proposed eight billion dollars in economic activity, and what enforceable mechanisms would have ensured that Baltimore residents—rather than external firms, property owners…
rows.full: Who specifically would have captured the proposed eight billion dollars in economic activity, and what enforceable mechanisms would have ensured that Baltimore residents—rather than external firms, property owners, consultants, and imported specialists—received a substantial share?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Who specifically would have captured the proposed eight billion dollars in economic activity, and what enforceable mechanisms would have ensured that Baltimore residents—rather than external firms, property owners…

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that scaling Baltimore’s innovation economy would automatically repair the social damage caused by previous industrial decline.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that scaling Baltimore’s innovation economy would automatically repair the social damage caused by previous industrial decline. Innovation growth can increase aggregate output while deepening spatial and racial inequality unless ownership, hiring, procurement, housing, and training institutions are deliberately structured around distribution.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that scaling Baltimore’s innovation economy would automatically repair the social damage caused by previous industrial decline.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Audit the original proposal as though it had been funded.
rows.full: Audit the original proposal as though it had been funded. Identify the projected recipients of each major expenditure, the evidentiary basis for job and growth estimates, resident-hiring obligations, employer demand, wage quality, ownership structure, displacement risks, and the commitments that remain valid without federal money. The decisive test is not whether Baltimore deserved investment, but whether the proposed institutional design would have converted investment into durable local power.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Audit the original proposal as though it had been funded.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: What minimum combination of provenance, date, hierarchy, geographic applicability, and surrounding context is required before a LOCUS chunk can support a legally meaningful claim about what governs a particular person, property, or institution?
rows.full: What minimum combination of provenance, date, hierarchy, geographic applicability, and surrounding context is required before a LOCUS chunk can support a legally meaningful claim about what governs a particular person, property, or institution?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What minimum combination of provenance, date, hierarchy, geographic applicability, and surrounding context is required before a LOCUS chunk can support a legally meaningful claim about what governs a particular person, property, or institution?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that the principal barrier to understanding local law is access to text.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that the principal barrier to understanding local law is access to text. Access is necessary, but the harder problem is authority: deciding which version, jurisdictional layer, exception, incorporated standard, or superior law controls. A searchable provision is not yet an answer.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that the principal barrier to understanding local law is access to text.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Conduct a stratified audit across states, vendors, cities, counties, OCR formats, taxonomy classes, and extreme normative scores.
rows.full: Conduct a stratified audit across states, vendors, cities, counties, OCR formats, taxonomy classes, and extreme normative scores. Have municipal-law experts and affected residents independently assess textual accuracy, currentness, jurisdictional applicability, function, topic, opacity, paternalism, discretion, and salience. Compare agreement not only with the trained classifiers but with the original GPT judgments. The decisive test is whether LOCUS measures properties of local law or primarily measures the interpretive system used to construct LOCUS.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Conduct a stratified audit across states, vendors, cities, counties, OCR formats, taxonomy classes, and extreme normative scores.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: How can a learner distinguish a genuinely causal anticipatory cue from a superficial correlation that happens to predict outcomes in familiar training conditions?
rows.full: How can a learner distinguish a genuinely causal anticipatory cue from a superficial correlation that happens to predict outcomes in familiar training conditions?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: How can a learner distinguish a genuinely causal anticipatory cue from a superficial correlation that happens to predict outcomes in familiar training conditions?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that more varied exposure will naturally yield better abstractions.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that more varied exposure will naturally yield better abstractions. Learners can instead encode irrelevant cues, stereotypes, or accidental regularities, particularly when feedback is delayed, ambiguous, or selectively remembered.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that more varied exposure will naturally yield better abstractions.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Compare experts and novices using temporally occluded scenarios that systematically remove or alter individual cues.
rows.full: Compare experts and novices using temporally occluded scenarios that systematically remove or alter individual cues. Measure prediction accuracy, confidence, gaze location, transfer to unfamiliar opponents, and adaptation after violated expectations. The decisive issue is not how many patterns a person has accumulated, but whether their internal chunks track stable causal structure rather than the surface regularities of a narrow environment.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Compare experts and novices using temporally occluded scenarios that systematically remove or alter individual cues.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: What makes an internal process a representation of a particular external process, rather than merely another process that happens to correlate with it?
rows.full: What makes an internal process a representation of a particular external process, rather than merely another process that happens to correlate with it?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What makes an internal process a representation of a particular external process, rather than merely another process that happens to correlate with it?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that preserving enough causal or relational structure is sufficient to produce meaning.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that preserving enough causal or relational structure is sufficient to produce meaning. That leaves unresolved who or what determines which correspondences matter, how mistaken representation remains representation, and how models become anchored to particular objects, purposes, or situations.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that preserving enough causal or relational structure is sufficient to produce meaning.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Construct experiments that separate genuine model-based anticipation from conditioned association.
rows.full: Construct experiments that separate genuine model-based anticipation from conditioned association. Train organisms or artificial systems on the same observable regularities, then alter hidden causal structure while preserving familiar surface cues. Test whether they can predict unseen consequences, choose among novel interventions, revise their representations after anomalies, and transfer knowledge across changes in appearance. The decisive evidence would be not successful repetition, but flexible behavior under counterfactual situations never directly experienced.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Construct experiments that separate genuine model-based anticipation from conditioned association.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Who is accountable when harmful behavior emerges from the interaction among model, system prompt, retrieved data, tool responses, routing policies, and human approval rather than from any single component?
rows.full: Who is accountable when harmful behavior emerges from the interaction among model, system prompt, retrieved data, tool responses, routing policies, and human approval rather than from any single component?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Who is accountable when harmful behavior emerges from the interaction among model, system prompt, retrieved data, tool responses, routing policies, and human approval rather than from any single component?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that harness complexity can indefinitely compensate for unreliable models.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that harness complexity can indefinitely compensate for unreliable models. Beyond some threshold, additional scaffolding may increase brittleness, latency, cost, and opacity faster than it improves performance, creating systems whose apparent competence depends on an unmaintainable web of exceptions.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that harness complexity can indefinitely compensate for unreliable models.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: Create deliberative trials that separate three questions often collapsed together: what evidence supports consciousness, what moral precautions uncertainty warrants, and which legal arrangements remain reversible. Use independently audited models, disclose developer incentives, test susceptibility to synthetic persuasion, and compare citizen assemblies with and without purported AI representatives. The decisive question is not merely whether humans can respectfully disagree about artificial minds, but whether they can construct a political process that is not itself engineered by the institutions seeking authority over those minds.

rows.full: The protected assumption is that code is merely an implementation detail. Burning the code safely requires first extracting that tacit knowledge into durable artifacts.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that code is merely an implementation detail.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Treat regeneration as a verified state transition.
rows.full: Treat regeneration as a verified state transition. Preserve the specification, decision history, test suite, schema history, runtime observations, dependency contracts, and prior implementation as separate evidence layers. Generate multiple implementations from the same specification and compare their observable behavior under adversarial, migration, failure, and performance tests. The decisive question is not whether code can be reborn from a specification, but whether the specification contains enough of the program’s theory for each rebirth to remain the same system.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Treat regeneration as a verified state transition.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: <Stop_Authority>: a role or mechanism empowered to halt the loop when its objective becomes invalid, its evidence degrades, its costs exceed value, or its actions produce cumulative harm.
rows.full: <Stop_Authority>: a role or mechanism empowered to halt the loop when its objective becomes invalid, its evidence degrades, its costs exceed value, or its actions produce cumulative harm. Scheduling explains how a loop continues; governance must explain why it stops.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Stop_Authority>: a role or mechanism empowered to halt the loop when its objective becomes invalid, its evidence degrades, its costs exceed value, or its actions produce cumulative harm.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Who judges the evaluator, and what happens when generation, evaluation, production behavior, and human expectations disagree?
rows.full: Who judges the evaluator, and what happens when generation, evaluation, production behavior, and human expectations disagree?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Who judges the evaluator, and what happens when generation, evaluation, production behavior, and human expectations disagree?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that work can be decomposed into discoverable tasks with independently verifiable outcomes.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that work can be decomposed into discoverable tasks with independently verifiable outcomes. Where objectives are political, interpretive, relational, or emergent, the loop may automate one contested definition of success while presenting it as neutral completion.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that work can be decomposed into discoverable tasks with independently verifiable outcomes.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Model the loop as a state machine with explicit transitions among discovery, assignment, generation, evaluation, persistence, scheduling, escalation, and termination.
rows.full: Model the loop as a state machine with explicit transitions among discovery, assignment, generation, evaluation, persistence, scheduling, escalation, and termination. For every transition, specify who or what possesses authority, what evidence is required, what state is preserved, what costs accumulate, and how a human can intervene. Then compare identical loop architectures operated by different people to identify where judgment—not machinery—produces divergent outcomes. The decisive question is not how to make agents run themselves, but how to preserve accountable human judgment once continuous generation becomes nearly free.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Model the loop as a state machine with explicit transitions among discovery, assignment, generation, evaluation, persistence, scheduling, escalation, and termination.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: What authority does A24 actually possess inside the partnership, and what decisions could it prevent rather than merely advise?
rows.full: What authority does A24 actually possess inside the partnership, and what decisions could it prevent rather than merely advise?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What authority does A24 actually possess inside the partnership, and what decisions could it prevent rather than merely advise?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: <Worker_Table>: a governing body representing performers, writers, cinematographers, editors, designers, animators, craftspeople, and other workers whose labor or prior work may train, guide, or be replaced by the resulting systems.
rows.full: <Worker_Table>: a governing body representing performers, writers, cinematographers, editors, designers, animators, craftspeople, and other workers whose labor or prior work may train, guide, or be replaced by the resulting systems.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Worker_Table>: a governing body representing performers, writers, cinematographers, editors, designers, animators, craftspeople, and other workers whose labor or prior work may train, guide, or be replaced by the resulting systems.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that participation by a culturally respected studio automatically transfers artistic values into the technology.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that participation by a culturally respected studio automatically transfers artistic values into the technology. The opposite is equally possible: the partnership may transfer technological priorities—scale, speed, automation, and cost reduction—into the studio.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that participation by a culturally respected studio automatically transfers artistic values into the technology.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: Publish the agreement’s operational terms: funding structure, intellectual-property ownership, training sources, consent rules, data retention, model access, labor safeguards, disclosure standards, commercial rights, and termination conditions. Then identify which decisions are advisory and which require joint approval. The decisive question is not whether A24 sits at the table, but who owns the table, sets the menu, and can refuse what is served.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: <Integration_Ledger>: a mandatory record of every form of labor and infrastructure required to make a system function-training, data preparation, monitoring, correction, customer assistance, exception handling, physical alteration, downtime recovery, emotional labor, and liability.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Integration_Ledger>: a mandatory record of every form of labor and infrastructure required to make a system function-training, data preparation, monitoring, correction, customer assistance, exception handling…

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Who possesses the authority to refuse an experiment when the people bearing its risks are not the people receiving its profits or directing its design?
rows.full: Who possesses the authority to refuse an experiment when the people bearing its risks are not the people receiving its profits or directing its design?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Who possesses the authority to refuse an experiment when the people bearing its risks are not the people receiving its profits or directing its design?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The report sometimes implies that making labor visible will help produce fairer design and compensation.
rows.full: The report sometimes implies that making labor visible will help produce fairer design and compensation. Visibility alone does not redistribute power. Institutions can fully understand that workers are absorbing technological failures and still continue the arrangement because the hidden subsidy benefits them.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The report sometimes implies that making labor visible will help produce fairer design and compensation.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Require an integration-impact assessment before adoption.
rows.full: Require an integration-impact assessment before adoption. Identify who selects the system, who must change their behavior, who performs repair and exception work, who loses discretion, who owns the generated data, who benefits from successful experimentation, and who pays when it fails. Workers and affected communities should possess decision rights-not merely opportunities to provide feedback. The decisive question is not whether AI works, but whose work is being counted as part of the AI and whose is being erased to make the technology appear autonomous.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Require an integration-impact assessment before adoption.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Who possesses legitimate authority to translate the Declaration’s abstract rights into enforceable obligations when citizens fundamentally disagree about what those words mean?
rows.full: Who possesses legitimate authority to translate the Declaration’s abstract rights into enforceable obligations when citizens fundamentally disagree about what those words mean?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Who possesses legitimate authority to translate the Declaration’s abstract rights into enforceable obligations when citizens fundamentally disagree about what those words mean?

rows.full: <Semantic_Infrastructure>: the courts, schools, movements, media institutions, legislatures, rituals, and public arguments through which inherited political terms acquire practical meaning.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Semantic_Infrastructure>: the courts, schools, movements, media institutions, legislatures, rituals, and public arguments through which inherited political terms acquire practical meaning.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that richer knowledge of word origins clarifies present political obligations.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that richer knowledge of word origins clarifies present political obligations. Etymology can expose forgotten possibilities, but it cannot decide among them. The move from linguistic history to justice always requires a contemporary normative judgment.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that richer knowledge of word origins clarifies present political obligations.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Trace each major term through three layers: historical origin, founding-era use, and institutional execution.
rows.full: Trace each major term through three layers: historical origin, founding-era use, and institutional execution. Ask who was included at each stage, which meanings were suppressed, what material arrangements followed, and which actors could revise them. The decisive question is not what “liberty” or “happiness” originally meant, but how a society converts inherited words into present capacities—and who is still denied the power to pursue them.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Trace each major term through three layers: historical origin, founding-era use, and institutional execution.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: <Deletion_Authority>: a role empowered to reject proposed features, terminate agent routines, remove low-value complexity, and declare that additional software should not be built. When production is abundant, subtraction becomes a primary engineering capability.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Deletion_Authority>: a role empowered to reject proposed features, terminate agent routines, remove low-value complexity, and declare that additional software should not be built.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Who remains responsible for the long-term consequences of features generated by temporary agent swarms after the originating engineer, product manager, model version, or customer request has disappeared?
rows.full: Who remains responsible for the long-term consequences of features generated by temporary agent swarms after the originating engineer, product manager, model version, or customer request has disappeared?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Who remains responsible for the long-term consequences of features generated by temporary agent swarms after the originating engineer, product manager, model version, or customer request has disappeared?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that the product funnel contains enough valuable opportunities to consume expanded engineering capacity.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that the product funnel contains enough valuable opportunities to consume expanded engineering capacity. Many products do not suffer from insufficient features; they suffer from incoherence, accumulated complexity, weak differentiation, and an inability to remove what no longer serves users.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that the product funnel contains enough valuable opportunities to consume expanded engineering capacity.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: Track the complete lifecycle of AI-accelerated work: idea source, customer evidence, specification quality, generated-code volume, human review time, defects, incidents, adoption, retention, support burden, maintenance cost, and eventual deletion. Compare teams that use new capacity to ship more features with teams that use it to simplify systems, deepen customer research, improve reliability, and retire debt. The decisive question is not whether Claude Code turns one engineer into three, but whether the organization has enough judgment to prevent those three virtual engineers from building the wrong thing at triple speed.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Track the complete lifecycle of AI-accelerated work: idea source, customer evidence, specification quality, generated-code volume, human review time, defects, incidents, adoption, retention, support burden, maintenance cost, and eventual deletion.

rows.full: If AI use is genuinely an ordinary productivity tool comparable to any other, why would accurate disclosure make a worthy game substantially less likely to succeed?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: If AI use is genuinely an ordinary productivity tool comparable to any other, why would accurate disclosure make a worthy game substantially less likely to succeed?

rows.full: <Provenance_Receipt>: a standardized record identifying generated assets, model providers, source rights, human contributors, consent status, and modifications without reducing the entire production to a stigmatizing yes-or-no label.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Provenance_Receipt>: a standardized record identifying generated assets, model providers, source rights, human contributors, consent status, and modifications without reducing the entire production to a stigmatizing yes-or-no label.

rows.full: The protected assumption is that production efficiency naturally benefits small developers. Savings may instead flow to publishers, platform owners, model vendors, and investors while increasing content supply, depressing prices, intensifying competition, and making independent visibility even harder.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that production efficiency naturally benefits small developers.

rows.full: Compare disclosed and undisclosed AI-assisted games while controlling for genre, price, studio size, marketing, review quality, and depth of AI use. Study whether the label itself depresses sales or whether it reveals underlying concerns about quality and production ethics. The decisive question is not whether Steam should punish AI, but what information players need to decide which forms of game production they are willing to support.

rows.full: Does the penalty arise from the disclosure label, visible deficiencies in the games, opposition to particular production practices, or a combination of all three?

rows.full: <AI_Use_Taxonomy>: a classification separating internal assistance, code generation, licensed asset generation, placeholder content, final visual assets, writing, music, synthetic performance, translation, and live generative systems.

rows.full: The protected assumption is that higher productivity improves a developer’s competitive position. rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: Run controlled storefront experiments in which identical games are presented with different levels of production disclosure, then measure wish lists, purchases, review rates, trust, perceived quality, and willingness to recommend. Separately compare games by the exact kind and extent of AI use. The decisive question is not whether an “AI stigma” exists, but what players believe the disclosure tells them about the people, labor, and care behind the game.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Run controlled storefront experiments in which identical games are presented with different levels of production disclosure, then measure wish lists, purchases, review rates, trust, perceived quality, and willingness to recommend.

rows.full: <Prompt_Test>: a set of representative cases used to determine whether the generated prompt reliably produces the intended result. Without tests, “better” means more polished rather than more effective.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Prompt_Test>: a set of representative cases used to determine whether the generated prompt reliably produces the intended result.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: The protected assumption is that the user’s initial goal is valid and merely needs translation. Often the real problem is upstream: the goal is contradictory, the requested artifact will not solve the problem, or the user lacks evidence needed to specify the task responsibly.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that the user’s initial goal is valid and merely needs translation.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Replace “write me a better prompt” with a staged operation: reconstruct the objective, expose assumptions, identify missing decisions, propose competing task formulations, define success tests, and only then generate the instruction.
rows.full: Replace “write me a better prompt” with a staged operation: reconstruct the objective, expose assumptions, identify missing decisions, propose competing task formulations, define success tests, and only then generate the instruction. Preserve reusable domain rules separately from task-specific context so each request begins fresh without discarding institutional knowledge. The decisive question is not whether Claude can write the prompt better, but whether the resulting prompt expresses a theory of the task that both the human and the model can inspect, challenge, and test.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Replace “write me a better prompt” with a staged operation: reconstruct the objective, expose assumptions, identify missing decisions, propose competing task formulations, define success tests, and only then generate the instruction.

rows.full: <Production_Ledger>: a record of model version, prompts, references, source rights, generated elements, human revisions, costs, failed generations, and consent status. Without it, controllability and responsible use remain marketing claims.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Production_Ledger>: a record of model version, prompts, references, source rights, generated elements, human revisions, costs, failed generations, and consent status.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: The protected assumption is that more content production creates more creative value. In an environment already saturated with video, the marginal synthetic clip may add volume without adding attention, understanding, memory, or cultural significance.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: The article supplies confident product specifications and forecasts but no independent tests, primary technical documentation, comparison protocol, pricing analysis, or disclosed commercial relationship with the repeatedly linked product site. It should be read as promotional framing rather than sufficient evidence that the claimed production threshold has been crossed.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: Test the system through a complete short-film workflow rather than a demo reel. Measure prompt-to-usable-shot ratio, continuity failures, revision locality, character persistence, sound editability, rights documentation, total compute cost, human labor, and audience recognition of synthetic artifacts. The decisive question is not whether Seedance can generate professional-looking video, but whether it can support an accountable and revisable filmmaking process without transferring hidden costs elsewhere.

rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: <Reality_Gap_Ledger>: a continuously updated record of which physical relations, environments, populations, edge cases, and intervention types the model reproduces accurately, approximately, or not at all.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: What evidence would distinguish Olympus from an interactive video generator that maintains short-term visual coherence but lacks durable causal state?
rows.full: What evidence would distinguish Olympus from an interactive video generator that maintains short-term visual coherence but lacks durable causal state?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What evidence would distinguish Olympus from an interactive video generator that maintains short-term visual coherence but lacks durable causal state?

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: The protected assumption is that learning to predict video is a sufficient pathway to learning the world.
rows.full: The protected assumption is that learning to predict video is a sufficient pathway to learning the world. Video contains rich evidence about motion and appearance, but it omits force, intention, ownership, risk, social norms, off-camera state, and many variables that matter to embodied action.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Most performance, cost, physical-accuracy, and future-product claims come from the founder, investors, or commercially aligned partners.
rows.full: Most performance, cost, physical-accuracy, and future-product claims come from the founder, investors, or commercially aligned partners. The article provides useful detail about strategy and financing, but limited independent evidence that the company has crossed from compelling video generation into reliable world simulation.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Most performance, cost, physical-accuracy, and future-product claims come from the founder, investors, or commercially aligned partners.

rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Evaluate Olympus through intervention, not spectatorship.
rows.full: Evaluate Olympus through intervention, not spectatorship. Place agents in identical initial states, vary one action, and test whether consequences remain consistent across time, viewpoints, and repeated trials. Measure object permanence, causal reversibility, collision integrity, spatial memory, rare-event coverage, controllability, inference cost, and transfer to physical systems. The decisive question is not whether the model can show a plausible world, but whether it can sustain a world that resists the generator’s temptation to rewrite reality whenever the next frame becomes difficult.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Evaluate Olympus through intervention, not spectatorship.

rows.kernel: <Adoption_Depth_Index>: a measure distinguishing incidental individual use from workflow assistance, partial automation, agentic execution, and organization-wide redesign, while recording the human labor and risk controls required at each level.
rows.full: <Adoption_Depth_Index>: a measure distinguishing incidental individual use from workflow assistance, partial automation, agentic execution, and organization-wide redesign, while recording the human labor and risk controls required at each level.

rows.full: Which companies should not adopt current AI systems, and what evidence would justify deliberate non-adoption?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: Which companies should not adopt current AI systems, and what evidence would justify deliberate non-adoption?

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: The protected assumption is that adoption is ultimately desirable wherever organizational barriers can be overcome. Some barriers may instead encode essential safeguards, tacit expertise, professional accountability, or limits on automation that should be preserved.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that adoption is ultimately desirable wherever organizational barriers can be overcome.

rows.label: MISSING

rows.full: Study matched organizations over time and separate four variables: model capability, management quality, integration labor, and workflow redesign. The decisive question is not why more companies have failed to adopt AI, but which institutional conditions make adoption produce durable value rather than merely shifting costs and risks out of view.

rows.full: <Apprenticeship_Replacement_System>: a deliberate mechanism through which young workers acquire tacit knowledge, judgment, customer exposure, and responsibility when routine entry-level tasks are automated.

rows.full: What happens to the supply of senior expertise once the current generation of experienced workers retires and the reduced junior cohorts fail to replace them?
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: What happens to the supply of senior expertise once the current generation of experienced workers retires and the reduced junior cohorts fail to replace them?

rows.full: The protected assumption is that removing routine work frees humans for more valuable work. For beginners, routine work is often the means through which they become capable of valuable work. Eliminating repetition can eliminate learning.
rows.visualSeed: Visualize as one stark diagram: The protected assumption is that removing routine work frees humans for more valuable work.

rows.full: Follow workers rather than occupations. Distinguish companies that use AI to eliminate junior roles from those that use it to accelerate supervised learning. The decisive question is not whether AI reduces entry-level employment today, but whether society can preserve the formation of expertise after the traditional ladder’s first rungs have been removed.

rows.full: The primary Forbes investigation is truncated at the paywall. rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Separate the capital narrative from the operating company.
rows.full: Separate the capital narrative from the operating company. For each startup, track cash raised, effective price by tranche, liquidation preferences, product availability, paying customers, retention, gross margin, inference expenditure, maintenance burden, and revenue concentration. rows.label: MISSING
rows.kernel: Evaluate world foundation models through controlled interventions rather than generated examples alone.
rows.full: Evaluate world foundation models through controlled interventions rather than generated examples alone. Give multiple models the same initial state, vary one action, and compare their predicted consequences with measured physical outcomes across unfamiliar objects, bodies, weather, damage, delayed effects, and human interruption. The decisive question is not whether a model can generate an infinite supply of worlds. It is whether those worlds fail in ways that are visible before a physical agent learns from them.

Test each on tasks requiring visual transformation, hidden force, hardware-specific control, delayed consequences, unfamiliar objects, social interpretation, and recovery from failed action. It is whether that imagination preserves enough of the particular body and world to make success physically achievable.
